companySlogan: We exist to elevate the industry through trust and uncommon success to the people we serve.
companyDescription:
As one of the fastest-growing Residential Solar organizations in the nation, Spartan Solar has pioneered a new method of partnership and collaboration in the industry. With a unique offer in every market they service, Spartan has something for everyone across the US.

Our bread-and-butter deal is simple: offer a top-of-the-line product at an unbeatable price. By exceeding expectations and providing incredible value, we aim to make it as easy as possible for a solar representative to do what they do best: solve problems.

We take pride in knowing that our organization is founded on a culture that demands the best out of one another every day. While financial success is something worth striving for, we believe it is second to personal excellence. To be a Spartan is to be challenged, and in overcoming adversity we become great.

title: Financial Controller
descriptionHtml:
Description

POSITION SUMMARY

The Financial Controller is a key financial leader responsible for all accounting, financial reporting, and internal control functions for a multi-state manufacturer and retailer of portable buildings, including a large and growing Rent-to-Own (RTO) portfolio. This role ensures the integrity of financial data, supports operational scalability, and provides leadership across manufacturing, retail, and RTO accounting functions. The Controller partners closely with the CFO and operational leaders to drive profitability, optimize working capital, and support strategic growth initiatives.

WHY YOU WANT TO WORK WITH US

This is a rare opportunity for a Controller to step into a high-impact role with an established, data-driven, vertically integrated business with 20+ years of success. You won’t be “just another number” here. You’ll be known, trusted, and valued as a finance leader, partnering with a cohesive, mission-driven team and supported by long-tenured employees who stay because the culture is genuinely exceptional.

Our family-oriented headquarters is in peaceful Western Kentucky at the confluence of five major rivers and near two of the largest lakes in the U.S. Centrally located between Nashville, St. Louis, and Memphis - with daily airline service to Chicago and Houston - this role offers an uncommon mix of professional influence, operational visibility, and quality of life. Perfect if you’re looking for stability, impact, and a team that backs you up. Opportunities like this don’t come along often.

Graceland offers a competitive salary, company sponsored insurance offerings, a 401(k) with company match, a generous paid time off allowance, paid holidays, and so much more.

Key Responsibilities

  • Lead the monthly, quarterly, and annual close processes ensuring accurate and timely financial reporting
  • Prepare consolidated financial statements across multiple entities and states in accordance with GAAP
  • Ensure audit-ready financials with strong documentation and reconciliation practices
  • Ensure proper financial controls
  • Establish accounting policies and procedures
  • Work closely with FP and A to produce actual, budgeted, and forecasted financial information
  • Oversee accounting for a large RTO portfolio, including:
  • Cost accounting for inventory
  • Revenue recognition for retail sales
  • Deferred revenue and receivables accounting
  • Portfolio aging, collections tracking, and reserve analysis
  • Establish and monitor allowance for doubtful accounts
  • Partner with CFO and Operations to optimize RTO portfolio performance and cash flow
  • Ensure proper accounting treatment within NetSuite, including system configuration and reporting
  • Lead all cost accounting functions for manufacturing operations
  • Standard costing and variance analysis
  • Bill of materials management
  • Labor and overhead allocation
  • Oversee inventory accounting
  • Data analysis
  • Inventory valuation
  • Fulfill multi-state compliance and audit requirements
  • Oversee coordination of tax filings
  • Lead and develop accounting team
  • Foster a culture of accountability, continuous improvement, and operational partnership

Requirements

QUALIFICATIONS

  • Bachelor's degree in Accounting or Finance
  • Master's degree in Accounting or CPA designation strongly preferred
  • 8-12+ years of progressive accounting/finance experience, including leadership roles
  • Prior experience in:
  • Manufacturing
  • Cost Accounting
  • Rent-to-Own, leasing, or installment based revenue models (preferred)
  • Multi-entity, multi-state operations
  • Highly proficient in Excel and financial modeling
  • Strong knowledge of GAAP, internal controls, and audit processes
  • Extensive hands-on experience with an ERP (preferably NetSuite)

PERSONAL ATTRIBUTES

  • Proven ability to lead teams in a fast-paced, growth-oriented environment
  • Strong analytical mindset with attention to detail
  • Ability to translate financial data into actionable business insights
  • Excellent communication skills across finance and operations teams

companySlogan: Look for the 'G'!
companyDescription: Graceland Management Services, LLC provides management services to the subsidiaries of Graceland Portable Buildings. Services include manufacturing, pre and post manufacturing services, property management, retail management, dealer management, RTO portfolio management, and a variety of other industry services. Graceland offers team members a competitive salary, an excellent benefit package, and a great work-life balance.

title: Marketing Campaign Specialist
descriptionHtml:
Summary

The Specialist, Campaigns & Platforms supports the Manager in the close coordination, synchronization, and execution of campaigns as part of the annual planning process. This role is responsible for assisting in the implementation, localization, and enrichment of International campaigns across all local channels, in collaboration with local teams. The Specialist also supports the development and implementation of local national platforms, campaigns, and projects, aids in the optimization of the campaign process, and helps to monitor and coordinate campaign aspects across all channels to ensure omni-channel implementation. Furthermore, the role assists with project management for local store marketing, including new and remodeled store openings, and supports the adaptation of International campaigns in the store.

What You’ll Do

Essential Functions

  • Implement and manage assigned programs, processes and initiatives through effective delegation and collaboration with stakeholders
  • Analyze and evaluate current processes and projects for recommended optimization
  • Provide creative input to design teams and maintain strong working relationships across internal departments
  • Manage project documentation
  • Clear communication of business and departmental strategy
  • Conduct market research and monitor competitive in-store reactions to help inform future local marketing tactics.

What You’ll Need

Required Knowledge, Skills, Abilities

  • Excellent with standard office software, specifically the Google Suite.
  • Strong research skills with the ability to analyze market trends and competitive landscapes to provide actionable insights.
  • Proven ability to manage multiple weekly priorities alongside ad-hoc tasks while meeting tight deadlines.
  • Foundational project management skills, including the ability to track timelines and follow through on multi-step deliverables.
  • Solid understanding of marketing principles and the ability to present creative direction or ideas clearly to the creative team.
  • A team-player mentality with the ability to work effectively in a highly collaborative, fast-paced environment.

Preferred Knowledge, Skills And Abilities

  • Experience leading end-to-end campaigns from research and strategy to execution across owned and paid channels.
  • Proficient in Google Sheets (data tracking/analysis/formatting/organization/formulas) and Google Slides (presentations)

Required Education, Certifications/ Licenses, Related Experience

  • Bachelor’s degree in marketing, economics or communication
  • 1-3 years of experience in a related field
  • In lieu of degree, 5+ years of experience in related field will meet the education and related experience requirements listed above

Physical Job Requirements

  • Ability to remain in a stationary position (standing and/or seated) more than half the time
  • Ability to maneuver cases of product (lift, move, carry, slide, etc.) up to 30 lbs.
  • Ability to identify and distinguish between colors more than half the time

Travel Requirements

  • This position commutes between stores, regional offices and/or distribution centers less than 25% of the time

What You’ll Receive

At Lidl, we know that in order for our people to do their best, they must be at their best. That’s why as a company, we offer one of the most generous benefits packages in the industry.

All our Lidl employees are eligible to receive the following benefits:

  • Medical & Prescription | Dental | Vision coverage
  • Paid Holiday & Paid Time Off (PTO) to use for sickness or vacation
  • Dedicated training plans to ensure you are set up for success
  • 401k Plan (+ 5% company match)
  • Voluntary Term Life & AD&D Insurance
  • Total Well-Being Program
  • DailyPay, a voluntary benefit to offer employees access to their pay on their own schedule

Lidl US views our Corporate Social Responsibility (CSR) through a lens of actions that are Good for the Environment, Good for People, and Good for You.
descriptionText:
Summary

The Specialist, Campaigns & Platforms supports the Manager in the close coordination, synchronization, and execution of campaigns as part of the annual planning process. This role is responsible for assisting in the implementation, localization, and enrichment of International campaigns across all local channels, in collaboration with local teams. The Specialist also supports the development and implementation of local national platforms, campaigns, and projects, aids in the optimization of the campaign process, and helps to monitor and coordinate campaign aspects across all channels to ensure omni-channel implementation. Furthermore, the role assists with project management for local store marketing, including new and remodeled store openings, and supports the adaptation of International campaigns in the store.

What You’ll Do

companyDescription:
Lidl is a family-owned business grounded on a simple philosophy: that customers deserve the highest quality goods and groceries at prices everyone can afford. For more than forty years, Lidl’s unique approach has changed and improved communities across 32 countries, where Lidl has grown to operate around 11,550 stores and employ more than 341,000 people.

In 2017, we brought our unique approach to the U.S. and opened our first stores in Virginia and the Carolinas. In less than three years, Lidl has won more than 500 awards for our unique products and shopping experience and has been recognized as a Top 3 U.S. supermarket by Food & Wine Magazine. Our U.S. footprint has grown over that same period to more than 150 stores across 9 East Coast states, with more than 5,000 U.S. team members supporting our growth.  

We are just getting started.  

Lidl US offers employees the opportunity to be a part of something big. Whether you want to join a team and be the best at what you do, or grow your career into a leadership position, Lidl US will support your efforts and create opportunities for you to develop your career – wherever it takes you. 

Are you up for it?
 
Be a part of something fresh, new and exciting! Check out all of our open job opportunities on careers.lidl.com.

title: Interior Designer
descriptionHtml:
Apply

Description

GMB is currently seeking a full time, Interior Designer to join our Team of Teams! This role will collaborate closely with architects, engineers, clients and contractors throughout project design.

Why work with us?

This is the part where most job descriptions jump into the responsibilities and requirements for the role being posted - and we'll get there shortly! But we like to start with the why instead of the what.

Our purpose is helping build strong communities through educational opportunity. We focus on projects within the educational ecosystem, from Pre-K through Higher Education and spaces in our community that support lifelong learning. Our integrated team gets to work together on a wide range of project types – building design, performance engineering, communications campaigns, branding, enrollment marketing, financial analysis, projections and planning – and we're proud of the impact that our work has on the lives and trajectories of students across the United States. Will you join us?

As An Interior Designer With GMB You Will

  • Collaborate with cross-disciplinary project teams on all aspects of interior architecture projects including programming, space planning, conceptual design, production of design development and construction documents for large complex projects.
  • Investigate, evaluate and recommend solutions that best meet the client's needs.
  • Develop design ideas, participate in coordination of project design solutions, and develop design documentation for both presentation materials and technical documentation.
  • Develop and produce construction documents, including detailing of interior materials & systems
  • Assist the project lead with the direction of the overall project process including development of work plans and proposals
  • Work independently and alongside team to meet project constraints of budget and schedule. Independently create and maintain timelines and budgets for FFE projects.
  • Complete project specifications by providing relevant and required product and equipment information and compiling and analyzing relevant data for interior components and FFE solutions.
  • Select, sample, develop and coordinate color & material palettes and finish and furniture selections for projects.
  • Coordinate installation of design elements and furnishings
  • Practice client relations and customer service throughout the design process
  • Undertake selection and research of project design materials, furnishings, fixtures, and equipment
  • Attend on-site visits to assist or lead collaboration with contractors and vendors
  • Effectively illustrate interior design ideas to the client and project team
  • Develop and lead effective client presentations.
  • Perform field verification and coordinate and document findings.
  • Perform site inspections, review shop drawings, material submittals and other related construction administration tasks

What type of knowledge do I need to succeed in this role?

More than a specific degree and exact number of years of experience, we're most excited about what kind of knowledge you bring. For this role, we are seeking someone with developing knowledge who can take on tasks with increasing ownership and work independently on many tasks.

Some components that may help indicate you are at this knowledge milestone include more tangible items like:

  • Bachelor's degree in interior design from an accredited design school
  • 2+ years of experience in an architectural or interior design firm
  • Working knowledge of design, trends, construction methodology, material application, and manufacturer-supplier appropriateness including furniture, fixtures, lighting, and visual art
  • Understand the architectural design process and integration of interior design concepts with ability to lead and develop design element components.
  • Working knowledge of architectural building systems, sustainability, and integrated design
  • Ability to consult with external clients and have excellent communication skills
  • Proficiency in Microsoft Office, CAD / Revit, and Adobe Suite
  • Proficiency in 3D modeling, visualization and graphic software(s).
  • Must have knowledge of current practices in the production of technical plans, specifications, drawings and models
  • Positive attitude with proven proficiency of working collaboratively and effectively within a team setting including architects, contractors, owners, consultants and vendors
  • Ability to work efficiently and with accuracy both independently and under supervision, and take responsibility for own work
  • Consistent background of strong team collaboration skills and results, with a proven ability to take the lead at times

What might set you apart from other candidates?

  • Experience with the K-12 and/or Higher Education market a plus
  • Experience with large projects, from $100k to $50M+
  • Experience with ACC/Build or other project information management software
  • Knowledge of LEED guidelines
  • Familiarity with SpecLink

We might be a great match for each other if you are:

  • A self-starter with great time management skills who thrives in an environment with team-based decision making.
  • An individual who strives to learn and grow, is motivated by their team's success and works hard to ensure it.
  • Someone who enjoys trying new ideas and constantly improving.

What You Might Also Like To Know Is That

We're an employee-owned company. This means that 100% of the ownership at GMB is held by us as employees. Our creativity, skills, and expertise have a direct impact on our value and growth. Your role as a teammate will have a direct impact on the company's actions and result in a share in its profits.

GMB doesn't operate within a hierarchical pyramid structure; instead, we operate like a network of teams that come together around specific goals working toward our overarching purpose. We think this is a better way to work and help everyone reach their potential.

Finally, at GMB we embrace a flexible workplace that empowers each person to decide where and how they work best. Our inclusive, people-first culture encourages teams to collaborate, communicate and find fulfillment personally and professionally, regardless of location or schedule.

This is a full time, permanent position. Salary will be commensurate with experience, including an excellent benefit package. Must have current and continuing right to work in the United States without sponsorship. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, age, sexual orientation, gender identity or national origin.

companySlogan: Design and manufacture collaborative robots (COBOTS) indigenously with the vision to enable widespread adoption.
companyDescription:
Systemantics is an Industrial Robotics company headquartered out of Bangalore, India. We are on a mission to enable widespread adoption of flexible automation in industry. With the experience of having implemented custom turnkey solutions for several clients, the company has accomplished several innovations in robotics and industrial automation such as under water robots and walking machines. 

Systemantics is focused exclusively on the design and manufacture of industrial collaborative robots (cobots) with innovations that facilitate affordable solutions and faster ROI.

title: Production Designer (Social/Digital Ads)
descriptionHtml:
This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for a Production Designer (Social/Digital Ads) in United States.

This role is focused on bringing digital and social campaigns to life through high-quality production design work across multiple formats and channels. You will translate established creative concepts into scalable, platform-ready assets for paid social, display advertising, and other digital environments. Working closely with art directors, designers, and copywriters, you will ensure consistency, precision, and visual excellence across all deliverables. The position is ideal for someone who enjoys detail-driven execution, fast-paced iteration, and maintaining strong design systems across large volumes of assets. You will play a key role in ensuring creative work is accurately adapted, resized, and optimized for performance across platforms. This is a remote opportunity requiring collaboration within PST business hours and offers exposure to a wide variety of brands and campaign types.

Accountabilities

  • Translate core creative concepts into multiple digital formats, adapting designs across various sizes, platforms, and channel requirements.
  • Produce final production-ready assets for paid social, display banners, app creatives, and other digital campaign deliverables.
  • Execute rapid design and copy updates while maintaining accuracy, consistency, and adherence to brand guidelines.
  • Collaborate closely with art directors, designers, and copywriters to implement feedback and refine creative outputs efficiently.
  • Prepare and deliver fully packaged final files for handoff and deployment across digital platforms.
  • Support occasional original design contributions beyond production tasks when needed.

Requirements

  • 2+ years of experience in production or graphic design within a creative agency, in-house studio, or similar environment.
  • Strong portfolio demonstrating digital banner production, paid social assets, and multi-format campaign adaptation.
  • Advanced proficiency in Adobe Creative Suite (Photoshop, Illustrator, InDesign) and strong working knowledge of Figma.
  • Excellent understanding of typography, layout, grid systems, and scalable design systems.
  • High attention to detail with a strong commitment to pixel-perfect execution and quality control.
  • Ability to manage multiple projects simultaneously while maintaining organization and deadlines.
  • Comfortable working independently as well as collaboratively in a feedback-driven environment.

Benefits

  • Fully remote position with the flexibility of working from India.
  • Opportunity to work on high-volume, large-scale digital campaigns for diverse global brands.
  • Exposure to fast-paced creative workflows within an international agency environment.
  • Collaborative team culture with close interaction with senior creative professionals.
  • Chance to strengthen expertise in production design systems and multi-platform advertising.
  • Work aligned with PST business hours, offering structured global collaboration.

How Jobgether Works

We use an AI-powered matching process to ensure your application is reviewed quickly, objectively, and fairly against the role's core requirements. Our system identifies the top-fitting candidates, and this shortlist is then shared directly with the hiring company. The final decision and next steps (interviews, assessments) are managed by their internal team.

We appreciate your interest and wish you the best!

Why Apply Through Jobgether?

Data Privacy Notice: By submitting your application, you acknowledge that Jobgether will process your personal data to evaluate your candidacy and share relevant information with the hiring employer. This processing is based on legitimate interest and pre-contractual measures under applicable data protection laws (including GDPR). You may exercise your rights (access, rectification, erasure, objection) at any time.

We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

companySlogan: Your career navigator for remote work.
companyDescription:
Jobgether is an AI-powered career coach and matching platform fixing the broken job search. Remote professionals no longer waste hours applying blindly; instead, they receive a personalized job search strategy, stronger visibility, and curated matches aligned with their skills, flexibility preferences, and career goals.

We flip the hiring model by connecting talent only to roles that truly match, reducing noise for employers and eliminating wasted effort for candidates. Jobgether combines AI coaching, profile optimization, Match Score insights, and the world’s largest remote job database to help people land opportunities faster and with less bias.

Our purpose is to make remote job search guided and intentional. 
Our mission is to become the world’s reference platform for remote talent, ensuring no professional remains invisible and every match is meaningful.

title: Motion Designer
descriptionHtml:
Please submit your application through this posting only. We're unable to review applications or follow-ups sent via email, direct message, or website inquiry.

About Nen Creative

Nen Creative partners with tech startups to launch bold ideas into the world, creating the brands, products, and stories that fuel their growth.

Since 2022, we've partnered with some of the world's top founders and institutions, including Amazon, Pear VC, Andreessen Horowitz, Mercor, Context Inc, Netic, Listen Labs, Tech Week, Mantis VC, Spur, Flora, and more.

We're growing fast and looking for great people to join us.

About The Opportunity

We're looking for a Motion Designer to join our growing team and help us bring our clients' biggest ideas to life in motion.

You'll work on launch videos, commercials, product explainers, social content, and brand campaigns for our roster of tech clients. The work spans 2D, UI, typography, compositing, and edit-driven animation — sometimes a polished hero sequence for a product launch, sometimes a fast-turn social cutdown, often both in the same week.

You'll partner closely with our producers, editors, designers, and directors, taking creative direction from our Motion Design Lead and contributing your own ideas as projects develop. You'll own your work from concept through final delivery, iterate quickly on feedback, and bring real craft to every frame regardless of timeline or scope.

This is a hands-on role in a fast-moving studio. We need someone with strong fundamentals, sharp visual instincts, and the appetite to push their work past the obvious version.

What You'll Do

  • Design and animate motion work across launch videos, commercials, product explainers, social content, and brand campaigns
  • Create work from concept through final delivery across 2D, UI, typography, compositing, and edit-driven animation
  • Translate creative direction and storyboards into compelling motion that hits the intended tone, pacing, and feel
  • Work closely with producers, editors, designers, and directors to bring ideas to life
  • Iterate quickly on feedback from creative leads and clients without losing the craft
  • Help build and maintain templates, presets, and motion systems that improve consistency across projects
  • Balance speed and quality while working across multiple projects and deadlines
  • Push work beyond the obvious. We value people with strong taste, attention to detail, and a desire to refine until the work feels exceptional
  • Stay current on tools, techniques, and trends that can elevate the motion work we ship

What We're Looking For

  • 3 to 5 years of experience as a motion designer, ideally in a studio, agency, or in-house creative team
  • A strong reel that demonstrates range across product and UI motion, brand work, and edit-driven animation
  • Expert in After Effects; comfortable in Figma and Premiere Pro or DaVinci Resolve
  • Strong visual design fundamentals — type, layout, color, composition, timing
  • Real point of view on motion — easing, transitions, restraint — and the ability to talk through your decisions
  • Solid eye for detail. You notice the things most people don't and care about getting them right
  • Comfortable taking feedback and iterating quickly without losing momentum
  • Experience in studio, agency, or fast-moving startup environments
  • Self-directed and remote-ready. You can manage your own workload, communicate clearly in writing, and stay aligned across time zones
  • Calm, organized, and dependable under pressure

Why Join Nen

  • Be part of a small, fast-growing creative studio trusted by world-class startups and investors
  • Work directly with the partners and creative leadership on ambitious, high-impact projects
  • Define and scale the motion practice as we build the team out
  • Medical, dental, and vision coverage, 401(k), PTO, and company holidays

companyDescription: Design studio that empowers early-stage companies to tell their story and build their vision. Capabilities include brand identity, pitch deck design/development, websites, and UI design. Clients include Pear VC, Andreessen Horowitz, LA-Tech Week, Wildr, Mantis VC, and more.

title: Administrative Assistant
descriptionHtml:
Orchestrate Consulting's mission is to help job seekers access carefully coordinated opportunities that match their career goals. We are not a staffing firm or agency. Orchestrate Consulting does not hire for these roles—we curate and validate them from verified employers.

Employer Industry: Accounting Services

Why Consider This Job Opportunity

  • Salary up to $26.00 per hour
  • Opportunity for career advancement and growth within the organization
  • Flexible office hours with summer hours allowing for earlier Fridays
  • Inclusive work environment that values diversity and fosters professional development
  • Chance to work with a supportive team and make a positive impact on client satisfaction

What To Expect (Job Responsibilities)

  • Process and e-file client tax returns and mailings as necessary
  • Provide dedicated partner support including time and expense entry, billing, and travel assistance
  • Assist in the preparation, proofreading, and processing of various documents to ensure error-free work
  • Organize and maintain calendars for assigned partners, coordinating logistics for meetings and conference calls
  • Maintain confidentiality and adhere to the highest professional standards in all communications

What Is Required (Qualifications)

  • Minimum of three years of relevant experience in an administrative role
  • Associate's degree or a high school diploma/GED with equivalent work experience
  • Knowledge of office methods, procedures, and business English usage, including spelling and grammar
  • Proficiency in Microsoft Word, Excel, Outlook, and PowerPoint
  • Ability to handle communications professionally with both internal personnel and external clients

How To Stand Out (Preferred Qualifications)

  • Experience in a tax or accounting environment
  • Strong organizational skills and attention to detail
  • Familiarity with client management software or tools
  • Ability to learn and adapt to new technology quickly
  • Previous experience providing administrative support to senior management

title: Director of Student Life
descriptionHtml:
At Pacifica Christian High School, we teach our students to think and live well.

Embark on a transformative journey with Pacifica Christian High School (Pacifica), where education is about more than achievement—it’s about joyful formation: teaching students to think and live well, to the glory of God. Founded in 2015 with 48 students, Pacifica has grown into a thriving community, with close to 400 students, and a growing alumni base already numbering in the hundreds. We are a young school with uncommon momentum and a future still being built.

Student life is central to this vision. Pacifica is a campus marked by joy, energy, tradition, and belonging. Signature experiences such as All-School Retreat, dances, pep rallies, Triton Ball, Wedge Week, First Friday, Friendsgiving, Christmifica, and more are not merely events on a calendar. They are formative opportunities for students to take risks, build friendships, deepen school spirit, grow in faith, and discover the joy of life together.

The Opportunity / Job Summary

Pacifica is seeking an entrepreneurial, highly relational, and strategically minded Director of Student Life to lead and strengthen the student experience across the school. This person will oversee student life programming, major events, student traditions, service learning, student-led clubs, and the House System, ensuring that each area supports Pacifica’s mission to free a generation to think and live well.

The Director of Student Life will work closely with Academic, Athletics, Arts, and Advancement leaders to cultivate a joyful, cohesive, and mission-aligned student culture. This role requires excellent planning, communication, organization, and program development skills, as well as a leader who loves teenagers, builds trust easily, and brings energy, creativity, flexibility, and excellence to the daily life of the school. The ideal candidate will see student life not as a collection of activities, but as a vital arena of Christian formation where students grow in faith, friendship, courage, service, leadership, and joy.

Essential Duties & Responsibilities

Daily/weekly duties include but are not limited to:

  • Co-Lead/Run Associate Students (AS) meetings during the week (2x a week)
  • Attend Executive Council Meeting every week. Oversee and attend all Major Student Life Events. (Friendsgiving, All School Retreat, Heritage Dinner, School Dances) related activities (not including athletics)
  • Attend select home & away sporting events to help promote school spirit, good sportsmanship, fair play, and a Christ-centered environment within the student body.
  • Attend select Art events to help promote school spirit, support and encourage student artists and ensure a Christ-centered environment within the student body.
  • Work with City and Government Offices, Local Businesses, Venues, Non-Profit Organizations, Pacifica Christian Athletics, Arts, Academics and other relatable organizations in coordinating student life events, functions, facility usage, on and off-campus, and all student life-related activities
  • Help oversee/coordinate school clubs on campus and everything relating to clubs
  • Maintain facilities and student life equipment/supplies to appropriate standards
  • Potentially lead/mentor a group of students in a weekly small group throughout the course of the year
  • Schedule meetings, when necessary, with Department Directors, Teachers, Students, Parents, and any other responsible party.
  • Provide support services and opportunities for all students to grow and develop as people.

Administrative Responsibilities

  • Oversee scheduling/coordinating of AS assemblies, pep rallies, special assemblies, and all related student life school meetings
  • Manage day-to-day expenses/finances in accordance with the Student-Life Event Budget and Director of Student Life approval
    • Work on the yearly Student Life Event Budget
    • Work on All School Retreat Budget
  • Update student life Resource Boards and all communication through MyPacifica when necessary.
  • Communicate student-life events with teachers, faculty, staff, parents, and students
  • Communicate all student life events with parents (via MyPacifica)
    • All Major Student Life Events must be posted on the “News - Recent Activity Page” of MyPacifca 4-5 weeks prior to the event. Events with a cost of $100 or more to the student body must be posted 6-8 weeks prior to the event
    • All Student Life activities must be put on the School Calendar and updated accordingly with accurate information
  • Coordinate and communicate with Pacifica Parent Partnership (P3)
    • The main contact for this is the Director of Advancement
  • Oversee and Coordinate the Prefect Program
  • Help coordinate and attend the Student Leadership Retreat in early August.
  • Help coordinate and attend the All-School Retreat
  • Help develop new programs and initiatives in areas such as:
    • Campus Life, House System, Community Mentor Programs, Service Learning, etc.
  • Perform any administrative tasks relevant to student life events or given by the Director of Athletics and Student Life
  • Confirm student life events planned week to week
  • Work with other department directors to ensure programmatic integration
  • Support other school activities through planning, participation and supervision
  • Represent the school publicly and assist the admissions process as needed
    • Student Ambassadors
    • High School Fairs
    • Open Houses
    • Shadow Days
Other Duties & Shared Responsibilities

  • Embodies and promotes Pacifica’s Core Beliefs and Approach to Teaching & Learning.
  • Wholeheartedly enjoys working with teenagers, championing their education, and encouraging their faith and Christian commitments.
  • Cultivates a vibrant culture of learning in and outside of the classroom and contributes to a healthy professional working community
  • Serves as an Advisor to a cohort of students.
  • Attends and participates in weekly Chapel and All-School Meeting as well as other school-day events/assemblies.
  • Celebrates students’ by attending their co-curricular activities on occasion.
  • Arrives on time, is well-prepared, acts professionally, and seeks to add value in every facet of the job while working collaboratively with colleagues and students to advance Pacifica's mission. Maintains communication with parents and responds to messages within 24 hours. Holds self directly accountable to their direct report and to the departments they serve, and ultimately the Head of School.
  • Attends and readily participates in Pacifica’s annual overnight All-School Retreat as well as Admissions Open House(s), Back-to-School Night, and Commencement activities.
  • Contributes to the supervision of a reasonable number of after-school activities as needed.

Requirements & Qualifications

  • Previous experience in youth ministries, educational leadership, school development, or a related field preferred
  • Bachelor’s degree from an accredited four-year college or university (Masters Degree in Education , Non-Profit Leadership, Educational Leadership, or a related field preferred)
  • Adequate knowledge and understanding of high school students
  • Requires standing for long periods of time
  • Requires lifting and moving of items upwards of fifty pounds
  • Must be CPR/First Aid Certified
  • Must be 21 years or older
  • Must have a valid CA Driver’s License
  • Must be willing to take and pass a background check
  • Workday starts at 9:00 am unless an event or meeting is taking place before 9:00 am
  • Flexible and adaptable in dealing with new, different, or changing requirements/situations
  • Effective in handling multiple concurrent tasks

Disclaimers

Pacifica Christian High School is an equal opportunity employer and is required by law to perform background checks on selected qualified candidates. All employees of Pacifica Christian must agree to carry out their responsibilities in a manner that is consistent with Pacifica Christians Mission Statement, Statement of Faith, and conduct themselves in a manner consistent with Biblical standards.

The above job description is meant to describe the general nature and level of work being performed; it is not intended to be construed as an exhaustive list of all responsibilities, duties and skills required for the position. Employees will be required to follow any other job-related instructions and to perform other job-related duties requested by their supervisor in compliance with Federal and State Laws.

Requirements are representative of minimum levels of knowledge, skills and/or abilities. To perform this job successfully, the employee must possess the abilities or aptitudes to perform each duty proficiently. Continued employment remains on an “at-will” basis.

All job requirements are subject to possible modification to reasonably accommodate individuals with disabilities. Some requirements may exclude individuals who pose a direct threat or significant risk to the health and safety of themselves or other employees and students.

title: Associate, Strategic Finance
descriptionHtml:
This is a hybrid position and you must be located in, or willing to relocate to New York for the position.

About The Role

As part of the Strategic Finance team, you will help scale the financial reporting, analytics, and planning capabilities and empower decision makers across our different businesses and functions. You will play a key role in all aspects of financial reporting and make significant contributions to high-profile strategic and operational initiatives. Your work and the insights you create will drive real time business decisions and help Uber to continue to grow, scale and become more profitable, while also creating more opportunities for the many thousands of courier, restaurants and drivers that use our platform daily.

We are seeking a detail-oriented and proactive Associate, Marketing Finance, to join our team in NYC. In this role, you will support the Global Head of Marketing Finance in consolidating and analyzing marketing investments globally, ensuring financial efficiency, and contributing to strategic decision-making processes.

What You'll Do

  • Financial & Operational Planning: Partner with company leaders to develop financial forecasts and operational goals
  • Partner with the business: To apply business judgment and financial insights to assist with decision-making related to new initiatives, products, and business opportunities
  • Sophisticated financial modeling: Break complex business problems down into components, and build models from scratch as well as improve existing models
  • Strategic Thinking & Execution Excellence: Partner with your team to execute projects and operationalize our insights
  • Work closely with accounting to provide input on management reporting needs and better understand our actuals
  • Be creative, adaptive, and eager to learn new technical skill sets

Basic Qualifications

  • BS/BA in Finance, Accounting, Economics, MIS or other relevant subject
  • 2+ years of proven experience
  • Experience with Excel/Google Sheets
  • Prior financial and/or analytical experience including FP&A, investment banking, management consulting, private equity, or operations

Preferred Qualifications

  • Outstanding analytical skills - standard methodologies are second nature, and your models are efficient, scalable, and intuitive
  • Excellent communication and people skills - numbers are key, but a business is built with people
  • Self-starter - you will take initiative, be dedicated, set and drive toward goals
  • Ability to prioritize - we are consistently working on multiple projects at any given time, so we are looking for someone who is comfortable working in an ambiguous environment and has the ability to optimally multitask
  • Standout colleague - this is a highly collaborative and encouraging team environment
  • Attention to detail and ability to execute in a fast-paced environment
  • Basic knowledge of GAAP
  • Experience with enterprise-level financial planning systems, SQL, and/or statistical software package

For New York, NY-based roles: The base salary range for this role is USD$106,000 per year - USD$118,000 per year. You will be eligible to participate in Uber's bonus program, and may be offered an equity award & other types of comp. All full-time employees are eligible to participate in a 401(k) plan. You will also be eligible for various benefits. More details can be found at the following link https://jobs.uber.com/en/benefits., For New York, NY-based roles: The base salary range for this role is USD$106,000 per year - USD$118,000 per year. You will be eligible to participate in Uber's bonus program, and may be offered an equity award & other types of comp. All full-time employees are eligible to participate in a 401(k) plan. You will also be eligible for various benefits. More details can be found at the following link https://jobs.uber.com/en/benefits.

companyDescription:
We are Uber. The go-getters. The kind of people who are relentless about our mission to help people go anywhere and get anything and earn their way. Movement is what we power. It’s our lifeblood. It runs through our veins. It’s what gets us out of bed each morning. It pushes us to constantly reimagine how we can move better. For you. For all the places you want to go. For all the things you want to get. For all the ways you want to earn. Across the entire world. In real time. At the incredible speed of now.

The idea for Uber was born on a snowy night in Paris in 2008, and ever since then our DNA of reimagination and reinvention carries on. We’ve grown into a global platform powering flexible earnings and the movement of people and things in ever expanding ways. We’ve gone from connecting rides on 4 wheels to 2 wheels to 18-wheel freight deliveries. From takeout meals to daily essentials to prescription drugs to just about anything you need at any time and earning your way. From drivers with background checks to real-time verification, safety is a top priority every single day. At Uber, the pursuit of reimagination is never finished, never stops, and is always just beginning.

title: Interior Designer
descriptionHtml:
Apply

Description

GMB is currently seeking a full time, Interior Designer to join our Team of Teams! This role will collaborate closely with architects, engineers, clients and contractors throughout project design.

Why work with us?

This is the part where most job descriptions jump into the responsibilities and requirements for the role being posted - and we'll get there shortly! But we like to start with the why instead of the what.

Our purpose is helping build strong communities through educational opportunity. We focus on projects within the educational ecosystem, from Pre-K through Higher Education and spaces in our community that support lifelong learning. Our integrated team gets to work together on a wide range of project types – building design, performance engineering, communications campaigns, branding, enrollment marketing, financial analysis, projections and planning – and we're proud of the impact that our work has on the lives and trajectories of students across the United States. Will you join us?

As An Interior Designer With GMB You Will

  • Collaborate with cross-disciplinary project teams on all aspects of interior architecture projects including programming, space planning, conceptual design, production of design development and construction documents for large complex projects.
  • Investigate, evaluate and recommend solutions that best meet the client's needs.
  • Develop design ideas, participate in coordination of project design solutions, and develop design documentation for both presentation materials and technical documentation.
  • Develop and produce construction documents, including detailing of interior materials & systems
  • Assist the project lead with the direction of the overall project process including development of work plans and proposals
  • Work independently and alongside team to meet project constraints of budget and schedule. Independently create and maintain timelines and budgets for FFE projects.
  • Complete project specifications by providing relevant and required product and equipment information and compiling and analyzing relevant data for interior components and FFE solutions.
  • Select, sample, develop and coordinate color & material palettes and finish and furniture selections for projects.
  • Coordinate installation of design elements and furnishings
  • Practice client relations and customer service throughout the design process
  • Undertake selection and research of project design materials, furnishings, fixtures, and equipment
  • Attend on-site visits to assist or lead collaboration with contractors and vendors
  • Effectively illustrate interior design ideas to the client and project team
  • Develop and lead effective client presentations.
  • Perform field verification and coordinate and document findings.
  • Perform site inspections, review shop drawings, material submittals and other related construction administration tasks

What type of knowledge do I need to succeed in this role?

More than a specific degree and exact number of years of experience, we're most excited about what kind of knowledge you bring. For this role, we are seeking someone with developing knowledge who can take on tasks with increasing ownership and work independently on many tasks.

Some components that may help indicate you are at this knowledge milestone include more tangible items like:

  • Bachelor's degree in interior design from an accredited design school
  • 2+ years of experience in an architectural or interior design firm
  • Working knowledge of design, trends, construction methodology, material application, and manufacturer-supplier appropriateness including furniture, fixtures, lighting, and visual art
  • Understand the architectural design process and integration of interior design concepts with ability to lead and develop design element components.
  • Working knowledge of architectural building systems, sustainability, and integrated design
  • Ability to consult with external clients and have excellent communication skills
  • Proficiency in Microsoft Office, CAD / Revit, and Adobe Suite
  • Proficiency in 3D modeling, visualization and graphic software(s).
  • Must have knowledge of current practices in the production of technical plans, specifications, drawings and models
  • Positive attitude with proven proficiency of working collaboratively and effectively within a team setting including architects, contractors, owners, consultants and vendors
  • Ability to work efficiently and with accuracy both independently and under supervision, and take responsibility for own work
  • Consistent background of strong team collaboration skills and results, with a proven ability to take the lead at times

What might set you apart from other candidates?

  • Experience with the K-12 and/or Higher Education market a plus
  • Experience with large projects, from $100k to $50M+
  • Experience with ACC/Build or other project information management software
  • Knowledge of LEED guidelines
  • Familiarity with SpecLink

We might be a great match for each other if you are:

  • A self-starter with great time management skills who thrives in an environment with team-based decision making.
  • An individual who strives to learn and grow, is motivated by their team's success and works hard to ensure it.
  • Someone who enjoys trying new ideas and constantly improving.

What You Might Also Like To Know Is That

We're an employee-owned company. This means that 100% of the ownership at GMB is held by us as employees. Our creativity, skills, and expertise have a direct impact on our value and growth. Your role as a teammate will have a direct impact on the company's actions and result in a share in its profits.

GMB doesn't operate within a hierarchical pyramid structure; instead, we operate like a network of teams that come together around specific goals working toward our overarching purpose. We think this is a better way to work and help everyone reach their potential.

Finally, at GMB we embrace a flexible workplace that empowers each person to decide where and how they work best. Our inclusive, people-first culture encourages teams to collaborate, communicate and find fulfillment personally and professionally, regardless of location or schedule.

This is a full time, permanent position. Salary will be commensurate with experience, including an excellent benefit package. Must have current and continuing right to work in the United States without sponsorship. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, age, sexual orientation, gender identity or national origin.

Independently create and maintain timelines and budgets for FFE projects.Complete project specifications by providing relevant and required product and equipment information and compiling and analyzing relevant data for interior components and FFE solutions.Select, sample, develop and coordinate color & material palettes and finish and furniture selections for projects.Coordinate installation of design elements and furnishingsPractice client relations and customer service throughout the design processUndertake selection and research of project design materials, furnishings, fixtures, and equipmentAttend on-site visits to assist or lead collaboration with contractors and vendorsEffectively illustrate interior design ideas to the client and project teamDevelop and lead effective client presentations.Perform field verification and coordinate and document findings.Perform site inspections, review shop drawings, material submittals and other related construction administration tasks

What type of knowledge do I need to succeed in this role?

More than a specific degree and exact number of years of experience, we're most excited about what kind of knowledge you bring. For this role, we are seeking someone with developing knowledge who can take on tasks with increasing ownership and work independently on many tasks.

Some components that may help indicate you are at this knowledge milestone include more tangible items like:

Bachelor's degree in interior design from an accredited design school2+ years of experience in an architectural or interior design firmWorking knowledge of design, trends, construction methodology, material application, and manufacturer-supplier appropriateness including furniture, fixtures, lighting, and visual artUnderstand the architectural design process and integration of interior design concepts with ability to lead and develop design element components.Working knowledge of architectural building systems, sustainability, and integrated designAbility to consult with external clients and have excellent communication skillsProficiency in Microsoft Office, CAD / Revit, and Adobe SuiteProficiency in 3D modeling, visualization and graphic software(s).Must have knowledge of current practices in the production of technical plans, specifications, drawings and modelsPositive attitude with proven proficiency of working collaboratively and effectively within a team setting including architects, contractors, owners, consultants and vendorsAbility to work efficiently and with accuracy both independently and under supervision, and take responsibility for own workConsistent background of strong team collaboration skills and results, with a proven ability to take the lead at times

What might set you apart from other candidates?

Experience with the K-12 and/or Higher Education market a plusExperience with large projects, from $100k to $50M+Experience with ACC/Build or other project information management softwareKnowledge of LEED guidelinesFamiliarity with SpecLink

We might be a great match for each other if you are:

A self-starter with great time management skills who thrives in an environment with team-based decision making.An individual who strives to learn and grow, is motivated by their team's success and works hard to ensure it.Someone who enjoys trying new ideas and constantly improving.

What You Might Also Like To Know Is That

We're an employee-owned company. This means that 100% of the ownership at GMB is held by us as employees. Our creativity, skills, and expertise have a direct impact on our value and growth. Your role as a teammate will have a direct impact on the company's actions and result in a share in its profits.

GMB doesn't operate within a hierarchical pyramid structure; instead, we operate like a network of teams that come together around specific goals working toward our overarching purpose. We think this is a better way to work and help everyone reach their potential.

Finally, at GMB we embrace a flexible workplace that empowers each person to decide where and how they work best. Our inclusive, people-first culture encourages teams to collaborate, communicate and find fulfillment personally and professionally, regardless of location or schedule.

This is a full time, permanent position. Salary will be commensurate with experience, including an excellent benefit package. Must have current and continuing right to work in the United States without sponsorship. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, age, sexual orientation, gender identity or national origin.
companySlogan: Design and manufacture collaborative robots (COBOTS) indigenously with the vision to enable widespread adoption.
companyDescription:
Systemantics is an Industrial Robotics company headquartered out of Bangalore, India. We are on a mission to enable widespread adoption of flexible automation in industry. With the experience of having implemented custom turnkey solutions for several clients, the company has accomplished several innovations in robotics and industrial automation such as under water robots and walking machines. 

Systemantics is focused exclusively on the design and manufacture of industrial collaborative robots (cobots) with innovations that facilitate affordable solutions and faster ROI.

title: Motion Designer
descriptionHtml:
Please submit your application through this posting only. We're unable to review applications or follow-ups sent via email, direct message, or website inquiry.

About Nen Creative

Nen Creative partners with tech startups to launch bold ideas into the world, creating the brands, products, and stories that fuel their growth.

Since 2022, we've partnered with some of the world's top founders and institutions, including Amazon, Pear VC, Andreessen Horowitz, Mercor, Context Inc, Netic, Listen Labs, Tech Week, Mantis VC, Spur, Flora, and more.

We're growing fast and looking for great people to join us.

About The Opportunity

We're looking for a Motion Designer to join our growing team and help us bring our clients' biggest ideas to life in motion.

You'll work on launch videos, commercials, product explainers, social content, and brand campaigns for our roster of tech clients. The work spans 2D, UI, typography, compositing, and edit-driven animation — sometimes a polished hero sequence for a product launch, sometimes a fast-turn social cutdown, often both in the same week.

You'll partner closely with our producers, editors, designers, and directors, taking creative direction from our Motion Design Lead and contributing your own ideas as projects develop. You'll own your work from concept through final delivery, iterate quickly on feedback, and bring real craft to every frame regardless of timeline or scope.

This is a hands-on role in a fast-moving studio. We need someone with strong fundamentals, sharp visual instincts, and the appetite to push their work past the obvious version.

What You'll Do

  • Design and animate motion work across launch videos, commercials, product explainers, social content, and brand campaigns
  • Create work from concept through final delivery across 2D, UI, typography, compositing, and edit-driven animation
  • Translate creative direction and storyboards into compelling motion that hits the intended tone, pacing, and feel
  • Work closely with producers, editors, designers, and directors to bring ideas to life
  • Iterate quickly on feedback from creative leads and clients without losing the craft
  • Help build and maintain templates, presets, and motion systems that improve consistency across projects
  • Balance speed and quality while working across multiple projects and deadlines
  • Push work beyond the obvious. We value people with strong taste, attention to detail, and a desire to refine until the work feels exceptional
  • Stay current on tools, techniques, and trends that can elevate the motion work we ship

What We're Looking For

  • 3 to 5 years of experience as a motion designer, ideally in a studio, agency, or in-house creative team
  • A strong reel that demonstrates range across product and UI motion, brand work, and edit-driven animation
  • Expert in After Effects; comfortable in Figma and Premiere Pro or DaVinci Resolve
  • Strong visual design fundamentals — type, layout, color, composition, timing
  • Real point of view on motion — easing, transitions, restraint — and the ability to talk through your decisions
  • Solid eye for detail. You notice the things most people don't and care about getting them right
  • Comfortable taking feedback and iterating quickly without losing momentum
  • Experience in studio, agency, or fast-moving startup environments
  • Self-directed and remote-ready. You can manage your own workload, communicate clearly in writing, and stay aligned across time zones
  • Calm, organized, and dependable under pressure

Why Join Nen

  • Be part of a small, fast-growing creative studio trusted by world-class startups and investors
  • Work directly with the partners and creative leadership on ambitious, high-impact projects
  • Define and scale the motion practice as we build the team out
  • Medical, dental, and vision coverage, 401(k), PTO, and company holidays

title: Associate Product Manager
descriptionHtml:
To get the best candidate experience, please consider applying for a maximum of 3 roles within 12 months to ensure you are not duplicating efforts.

About Futureforce University Recruiting

Our Futureforce University Recruiting program is dedicated to attracting, retaining and cultivating talent. Our interns and new graduates work on real projects that affect how our business runs, giving them the opportunity to make a tangible impact on the future of our company. With offices all over the world, our recruits have the chance to collaborate and connect with fellow employees on a global scale. We offer job shadowing, mentorship programs, talent development courses, and much more.

Job Category

Product

Job Details

About Salesforce

Salesforce is the #1 AI CRM, where humans with agents drive customer success together. Here, ambition meets action. Tech meets trust. And innovation isn’t a buzzword — it’s a way of life. The world of work as we know it is changing and we're looking for Trailblazers who are passionate about bettering business and the world through AI, driving innovation, and keeping Salesforce's core values at the heart of it all.

Ready to level-up your career at the company leading workforce transformation in the agentic era? You’re in the right place! Agentforce is the future of AI, and you are the future of Salesforce.

Please note: This posting is designated for a selected candidate and is not part of the general Associate Product Manager application process.

About The Associate Product Manager Program

Calling all Trailblazers to join the Salesforce Product Management team! Trailblazers drive innovation, disrupt industries, and shape the world around us. Whether it’s delivering awesome user experiences, accelerating productivity, or improving their business acumen, Trailblazers propel the future of technology.

At Salesforce, nothing is more important than the success of our customers, and the Product organization has been focused on delivering customer success since Day 1. We deeply understand our customers and constantly seek their input on our products and gather their feedback to improve what we do. We rely on data, experimentation, and research to validate our assumptions and design the best product. We strategically align the company around the vision for market-leading products that our customers love. We partner with engineering, UX, data science, research, marketing, finance, and customer success allow us to rapidly deliver innovative products and next-generation technologies that change how our customers connect to their customers. Our B2B focus creates unique and exciting challenges for delivering the future of technology at scale, requiring us to package and design our products for adoption by thousands of top companies across the world.

We do all of this while giving back to the communities where we work and live. With our 1-1-1 Model we give 1% of our time, 1% of our product, and 1% of our equity back into the community. We believe that business is the greatest platform for change and are working toward a more equal world. And with our partners, customers, and Trailhead learning platform we are creating millions of jobs and skilled workers around the world.

As an APM, you can be part of shaping the future of technology with us. You will have the opportunity to join our product organization in an intensive 2 year rotational experience that allows you to develop your skills as a product manager and learn to PM the Salesforce way. During the 2 year program, you will gain hands-on experience managing products for several different teams, get direct exposure to senior executives and how our business is run, work with a formal mentor to help you build your career, participate in various trainings and events, and attend offsites.

Responsibilities:

  • Understand and analyze user needs.
  • Analyze market trends, competitors, customer research, and product data to drive the overall success of the product.
  • Help to define product vision and strategy.
  • Partner with designers to create an intuitive and beautiful user experience.
  • Work with engineers to build and launch new features.
  • Help evangelize product vision through presentations to stakeholders.

Experience/Skills Required:

  • Bachelor's or Master's Engineering, Computer Science, or a related field.
  • Previous industry internship in product management.

Experience/Skills Desired:

  • Proven ability to deliver on commitments.
  • Ability to be a self-starter, thrive under pressure, and manage multiple projects simultaneously.
  • Excellent analytical, problem-solving, organizational, presentation, interpersonal, written and verbal communication skills.
  • Deep interest in creating and analyzing products.
  • Product management or design experience with a focus on software products and technologies.

About Futureforce University Recruiting

Our Futureforce University Recruiting program is dedicated to attracting, retaining and cultivating talent. Our interns and new graduates work on real projects that affect how our business runs, giving them the opportunity to make a tangible impact on the future of our company.

With offices all over the world, our recruits have the chance to collaborate and connect with fellow employees on a global scale. We offer job shadowing, mentorship programs, talent development courses, and much more.

Unleash Your Potential

When you join Salesforce, you’ll be limitless in all areas of your life. Our benefits and resources support you to find balance and be your best, and our AI agents accelerate your impact so you can do your best. Together, we’ll bring the power of Agentforce to organizations of all sizes and deliver amazing experiences that customers love. Apply today to not only shape the future — but to redefine what’s possible — for yourself, for AI, and the world.

Accommodations

If you need a reasonable accommodation during the application or the recruiting process, please submit a request via this Accommodations Request Form.

Please note that Salesforce uses artificial intelligence (AI) tools to help our recruiters assess and evaluate candidates’ resumes and qualifications throughout the recruiting process. Humans will always make any candidate selection and hiring decisions. Please see our Candidate Privacy Statement for more information about how we use your personal data and your rights, including with regard to use of AI tools and opt out options.

Posting Statement

Salesforce is an equal opportunity employer and maintains a policy of non-discrimination with all employees and applicants for employment. What does that mean exactly? It means that at Salesforce, we believe in equality for all. And we believe we can lead the path to equality in part by creating a workplace that’s inclusive, and free from discrimination. Know your rights: workplace discrimination is illegal. Any employee or potential employee will be assessed on the basis of merit, competence and qualifications – without regard to race, religion, color, national origin, sex, sexual orientation, gender expression or identity, transgender status, age, disability, veteran or marital status, political viewpoint, or other classifications protected by law. This policy applies to current and prospective employees, no matter where they are in their Salesforce employment journey. It also applies to recruiting, hiring, job assignment, compensation, promotion, benefits, training, assessment of job performance, discipline, termination, and everything in between. Recruiting, hiring, and promotion decisions at Salesforce are fair and based on merit. The same goes for compensation, benefits, promotions, transfers, reduction in workforce, recall, training, and education.

In the United States, compensation offered will be determined by factors such as location, job level, job-related knowledge, skills, and experience. Certain roles may be eligible for incentive compensation, equity, and benefits. Salesforce offers a variety of benefits to help you live well including: time off programs, medical, dental, vision, mental health support, paid parental leave, life and disability insurance, 401(k), and an employee stock purchasing program. More details about company benefits can be found at the following link: https://www.salesforcebenefits.com.Pursuant to the San Francisco Fair Chance Ordinance and the Los Angeles Fair Chance Initiative for Hiring, Salesforce will consider for employment qualified applicants with arrest and conviction records.

At Salesforce, we believe in equitable compensation practices that reflect the dynamic nature of labor markets across various regions. The typical base salary range for this position is $132,000 - $146,000 annually. In select cities within the San Francisco and New York City metropolitan area, the base salary range for this role is $159,000 - $159,000 annually. The range represents base salary only, and does not include company bonus, incentive for sales roles, equity or benefits, as applicable.

title: QA Analyst
descriptionHtml:
ComboCurve is a industry leading cloud-based software solution for A&D, reservoir management, and forecasting in the energy sector. Our platform empowers professionals to evaluate assets, optimize workflows, and manage reserves efficiently, all in one integrated environment.

By streamlining data integration and enhancing collaboration, we help operators, engineers, and financial teams make informed decisions faster. Trusted by top energy companies, ComboCurve delivers real-time analytics and exceptional user support, with a world-class customer experience team that responds to inquiries in under 5 minutes.

We’re looking for a QA Analyst to support product quality as we ship new features. You'll work closely with engineers and product managers to validate workflows, test new releases, and help ensure our customers can trust ComboCurve's outputs — including our AI-powered agents and tools.

We're looking for someone with a solid foundation in QA and a willingness to grow into automation. You don't need to be an automation expert, but comfort picking it up on the job is important since it will be a core part of the role. Prior experience testing AI agents or chatbots is a plus, as is any exposure to reservoir engineering platforms like ComboCurve, ARIES, or PHDWin.

This is a great fit for someone a few years into their QA career who's ready to take on new challenges in a fast-moving, AI-focused environment.

What You’ll Do

  • Test new features and bug fixes across core web app workflows and dashboards.
  • Follow and improve regression test checklists during release cycles.
  • Help write clear test cases and lightweight documentation for recurring QA processes.
  • Log bugs with strong reproduction steps, screenshots, and “expected vs. actual” notes.
  • Partner with engineers to understand features and identify edge cases early.
  • Validate data accuracy and UI behavior in key analytical outputs.
  • Learn basic testing and automation tools over time (if/when needed).
  • Support incident follow-ups by re-testing fixes and helping prevent regressions.

Requirements

  • Experience with QA concepts, test case writing, or structured troubleshooting.
  • Strong attention to detail and ability to methodically work through test steps.
  • Comfort learning new tools and navigating modern web applications.
  • Clear written communication, especially for documenting steps and reporting issues.

Nice to Have / Pluses

  • Some knowledge or experience with ComboCurve.
  • Familiarity with reservoir engineering tools such as ARIES and/or PHDWin.
  • Experience in QA automation
  • Experience in testing AI agents/chatbots

title: Remote Medical Scheduling Specialist - Patient Access Center
descriptionHtml:
As a Remote Scheduling Specialist at Community Health Systems (CHS) - Patient Access Center, you’ll play a vital role in quality healthcare, building enduring relationships with our patients, and providing value for the people and communities we serve.

Our Team Members Enjoy a Robust Benefits Package Including

  • Paid Time Off (PTO)
  • Comprehensive Health Benefits - Medical, Dental & Vision
  • 401k with company match
  • Tuition reimbursement

Job Summary

The Scheduling Specialist is responsible for supporting scheduling functions across assigned hospitals, clinics, or centralized patient access centers and will be the first point of contact for patients. This focuses on managing patient appointment scheduling, helping with general patient needs, and accurately communicating patient needs to the clinical staff through centralized call center operations. The Scheduling Specialist ensures communications and appointments are accurate, timely, and compliant with organizational policies while fostering effective communication with clinicians, patients, and leadership.

Essential Functions

  • Completes accurate patient appointment scheduling across multiple clinics, depending on assignment.
  • Receives inbound communication from clinicians, patients, and staff via phone, text, email, and/or call center platforms to address scheduling needs, and handle urgent or emergent requests.
  • Assesses caller needs to identify urgent clinical matters for immediate warm transfer to clinic staff. For non-urgent requests (refills, clinical questions), accurately documents and route communications to the appropriate staff via the EMR.
  • Verifies patient demographics and insurance information, ensuring compliance with applicable requirements.
  • Research patient requests within the medical record, provide necessary information, and resolve inquiries effectively while maintaining patient confidentiality.
  • Monitors EMR in-baskets, call center systems, and related technology (as needed) to manage communication workflows effectively.
  • Provides timely and professional service to patients, providers, and facility staff, ensuring positive experiences and adherence to standards.
  • Performs other duties as assigned.
  • Complies with all policies and standards.
  • This is a fully remote opportunity.

Qualifications

  • H.S. Diploma or GED required
  • Bachelor's Degree in Healthcare Administration, Business Administration, or a related field preferred
  • 1-3 years of experience in scheduling, operations, or healthcare administration required
  • 1-3 years of experience in physician/provider scheduling, patient appointment scheduling, or call center operations

Knowledge, Skills And Abilities

  • Proficiency in scheduling software, EMR systems, and Microsoft Office Suite.
  • Excellent verbal and written communication skills with strong customer service orientation.
  • Delivers prompt, courteous, and knowledgeable support to customers.
  • Strong problem-solving skills and attention to detail.
  • Ability to manage multiple priorities in fast-paced hospital, clinic, or call center environments.
  • Knowledge of healthcare industry standards, patient confidentiality, and compliance protocols.

We know it’s not just about finding a job. It’s about finding a place where you are respected, valued and where your work is purposeful and fulfilling. A place where your talent is recognized, professional development is encouraged and career advancement is possible.

Community Health Systems is one of the nation's leading healthcare providers. With healthcare delivery systems in 36 distinct markets across 14 states, CHS operates 69 affiliated hospitals with more than 10,000 beds and approximately 1,000 other sites of care, including physician practices, urgent care centers, freestanding emergency departments, imaging centers, cancer centers, and ambulatory surgery centers.

This position is not eligible for immigration sponsorship now or in the future. Applicants must be authorized to work in the U.S. for any employer.
