title: Email Marketing Manager
descriptionHtml:
This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for an Email Marketing Manager in United States.

This role is a key driver of audience growth and engagement within a fast-paced digital media environment, where email serves as one of the most important channels for subscriber acquisition, retention, and monetization. The Email Marketing Manager will own end-to-end strategy and execution across a diverse portfolio of newsletters, ensuring strong performance across the entire lifecycle funnel. You will play a central role in shaping how audiences discover, engage with, and derive value from email content. The position blends strategic ownership with hands-on campaign execution, requiring strong analytical thinking and creative marketing skills. You will collaborate closely with editorial, advertising operations, and marketing teams to align email initiatives with broader business goals. This is a high-visibility role where your work directly influences audience growth, engagement, and revenue outcomes.

Accountabilities

  • Own and optimize the full email lifecycle strategy, including acquisition, onboarding, engagement, retention, reactivation, and win-back campaigns
  • Define and monitor key performance indicators across the email funnel, identifying trends and continuously improving campaign effectiveness
  • Design and execute segmentation strategies, behavioral targeting, automation workflows, and A/B testing frameworks to improve engagement and conversions
  • Partner with editorial and marketing teams to create integrated, cross-channel campaigns informed by subscriber behavior and email insights
  • Collaborate with AdOps teams to support newsletter sponsorships, advertising integrations, and revenue performance tracking
  • Analyze campaign performance using analytics tools and translate insights into actionable optimization strategies
  • Ensure email content aligns with brand voice, editorial standards, and quality expectations across multiple publications
  • Present performance reports, strategic recommendations, and growth insights to senior leadership and stakeholders
  • Mentor and support junior team members, providing guidance on execution, priorities, and professional development

Requirements

  • 3-5 years of experience in email marketing, lifecycle marketing, or newsletter operations with ownership of campaign performance
  • At least 1 year of experience managing or mentoring team members in a marketing or operations environment
  • Proven ability to develop and execute email growth strategies that drive acquisition, engagement, and retention
  • Hands-on experience with email marketing automation tools such as HubSpot, Mailchimp, Marketo, Sailthru, or similar platforms
  • Strong expertise in segmentation, automation workflows, behavioral targeting, and A/B testing methodologies
  • Experience analyzing campaign performance using tools such as Google Analytics, Looker, or similar BI/reporting platforms
  • Strong writing and editing skills with the ability to craft compelling email content and high-performing CTAs
  • Experience working cross-functionally with editorial, marketing, and advertising operations teams
  • Familiarity with CRM systems, CDPs, or audience data tools used for segmentation and targeting
  • Ability to present insights and strategies clearly to senior stakeholders while supporting junior team development
  • Experience supporting newsletter monetization initiatives such as sponsorships, affiliate programs, or ad integrations

Benefits

  • Competitive annual salary range of 75,000 to 85,000 USD
  • Flexible PTO policy allowing time off when needed
  • Remote-first work environment with full work-from-home flexibility
  • Comprehensive health, dental, and vision insurance for employees and families
  • 401(k) retirement plan with company matching contributions
  • Paid parental leave to support family needs
  • Wellness benefits including fitness reimbursements and Headspace access
  • Life, AD&D, short-term, and long-term disability insurance coverage
  • Career development programs, mentorship opportunities, and leadership growth initiatives
  • Book reimbursement and learning support benefits
  • Additional perks such as pet insurance and speaker series rewards

How Jobgether Works

We use an AI-powered matching process to ensure your application is reviewed quickly, objectively, and fairly against the role's core requirements. Our system identifies the top-fitting candidates, and this shortlist is then shared directly with the hiring company. The final decision and next steps (interviews, assessments) are managed by their internal team.

We appreciate your interest and wish you the best!

Why Apply Through Jobgether?

Data Privacy Notice: By submitting your application, you acknowledge that Jobgether will process your personal data to evaluate your candidacy and share relevant information with the hiring employer. This processing is based on legitimate interest and pre-contractual measures under applicable data protection laws (including GDPR). You may exercise your rights (access, rectification, erasure, objection) at any time.

We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

This role is a key driver of audience growth and engagement within a fast-paced digital media environment, where email serves as one of the most important channels for subscriber acquisition, retention, and monetization. The Email Marketing Manager will own end-to-end strategy and execution across a diverse portfolio of newsletters, ensuring strong performance across the entire lifecycle funnel. You will play a central role in shaping how audiences discover, engage with, and derive value from email content. The position blends strategic ownership with hands-on campaign execution, requiring strong analytical thinking and creative marketing skills. You will collaborate closely with editorial, advertising operations, and marketing teams to align email initiatives with broader business goals. This is a high-visibility role where your work directly influences audience growth, engagement, and revenue outcomes.

Accountabilities

Own and optimize the full email lifecycle strategy, including acquisition, onboarding, engagement, retention, reactivation, and win-back campaignsDefine and monitor key performance indicators across the email funnel, identifying trends and continuously improving campaign effectivenessDesign and execute segmentation strategies, behavioral targeting, automation workflows, and A/B testing frameworks to improve engagement and conversionsPartner with editorial and marketing teams to create integrated, cross-channel campaigns informed by subscriber behavior and email insightsCollaborate with AdOps teams to support newsletter sponsorships, advertising integrations, and revenue performance trackingAnalyze campaign performance using analytics tools and translate insights into actionable optimization strategiesEnsure email content aligns with brand voice, editorial standards, and quality expectations across multiple publicationsPresent performance reports, strategic recommendations, and growth insights to senior leadership and stakeholdersMentor and support junior team members, providing guidance on execution, priorities, and professional development

Requirements

3-5 years of experience in email marketing, lifecycle marketing, or newsletter operations with ownership of campaign performanceAt least 1 year of experience managing or mentoring team members in a marketing or operations environmentProven ability to develop and execute email growth strategies that drive acquisition, engagement, and retentionHands-on experience with email marketing automation tools such as HubSpot, Mailchimp, Marketo, Sailthru, or similar platformsStrong expertise in segmentation, automation workflows, behavioral targeting, and A/B testing methodologiesExperience analyzing campaign performance using tools such as Google Analytics, Looker, or similar BI/reporting platformsStrong writing and editing skills with the ability to craft compelling email content and high-performing CTAsExperience working cross-functionally with editorial, marketing, and advertising operations teamsFamiliarity with CRM systems, CDPs, or audience data tools used for segmentation and targetingAbility to present insights and strategies clearly to senior stakeholders while supporting junior team developmentExperience supporting newsletter monetization initiatives such as sponsorships, affiliate programs, or ad integrations

Benefits

Competitive annual salary range of 75,000 to 85,000 USDFlexible PTO policy allowing time off when neededRemote-first work environment with full work-from-home flexibilityComprehensive health, dental, and vision insurance for employees and families401(k) retirement plan with company matching contributionsPaid parental leave to support family needsWellness benefits including fitness reimbursements and Headspace accessLife, AD&D, short-term, and long-term disability insurance coverageCareer development programs, mentorship opportunities, and leadership growth initiativesBook reimbursement and learning support benefitsAdditional perks such as pet insurance and speaker series rewards

How Jobgether Works

We use an AI-powered matching process to ensure your application is reviewed quickly, objectively, and fairly against the role's core requirements. Our system identifies the top-fitting candidates, and this shortlist is then shared directly with the hiring company. The final decision and next steps (interviews, assessments) are managed by their internal team.

We appreciate your interest and wish you the best!

 Why Apply Through Jobgether?

Data Privacy Notice: By submitting your application, you acknowledge that Jobgether will process your personal data to evaluate your candidacy and share relevant information with the hiring employer. This processing is based on legitimate interest and pre-contractual measures under applicable data protection laws (including GDPR). You may exercise your rights (access, rectification, erasure, objection) at any time.

We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.
companySlogan: Your career navigator for remote work.
companyDescription:
Jobgether is an AI-powered career coach and matching platform fixing the broken job search. Remote professionals no longer waste hours applying blindly; instead, they receive a personalized job search strategy, stronger visibility, and curated matches aligned with their skills, flexibility preferences, and career goals.

We flip the hiring model by connecting talent only to roles that truly match, reducing noise for employers and eliminating wasted effort for candidates. Jobgether combines AI coaching, profile optimization, Match Score insights, and the world’s largest remote job database to help people land opportunities faster and with less bias.

Our purpose is to make remote job search guided and intentional. 
Our mission is to become the world’s reference platform for remote talent, ensuring no professional remains invisible and every match is meaningful.

title: Freelance Junior Journalist - AI Trainer
descriptionHtml:
Please submit your resume in English and indicate your level of English proficiency.

Mindrift connects specialists with project-based AI opportunities for leading tech companies, focused on testing, evaluating, and improving AI systems. Participation isproject-based, not permanent employment.

What This Opportunity Involves

As an AI Trainer - Junior Journalist, your work will help train AI models, shaping how they understand and generate human-like text. This isn't just traditional writing—you'll be crafting text that teaches AI reasoning, logic accuracy, nuance, and clarity.

While each project involves unique tasks, contributors may:

  • Craft original, clear, and fact-checked responses based on project guidelines.
  • Generate prompts that challenge AI.
  • Define comprehensive scoring criteria to evaluate the accuracy of the AI's answers.
  • Follow style and quality standards to ensure consistency

What We Look For

This opportunity is a good fit if you are seeking for open to part-time, non-permanent projects. Ideally, contributors will have:

  • Bachelor's degree in Journalism, Communications, Linguistics, Literature, or Education to ensure a strong understanding of grammar and stylistic features;
  • At least 1 year of professional experience in Journalism, Communications, PR, etc., with strong skills in critical thinking and working with text in English language;
  • Strong written English (C1/C2);
  • Stable internet connection.

How It Works

Apply → Pass qualification(s) → Join a project → Complete tasks → Get paid

Project time expectations

For this project, tasks are estimated to require around 10-20 hours per week during active phases, based on project requirements. This is an estimate, not a guaranteed workload, and applies only while the project is active.

Compensation

On this project, contributors can earn up to $30 per hour equivalent, depending on their level and pace of contribution.

Compensation varies across projects depending on scope, complexity, and required expertise. Please note that other projects on the platform may offer different earning levels based on their requirements.

companySlogan: Be the “I” in AI with Mindrift. Work from anywhere on groundbreaking AI projects
companyDescription:
Mindrift (mindrift.ai) is the expert-sourcing platform built by Toloka, a Nebius Group (Nasdaq: NBIS) company and a global leader in data services for AI since 2014. Mindrift’s CEO and co-founder, Olga Megorskaya, launched the platform to connect domain experts — from software engineers and physicians to linguists and lawyers — with AI training projects for the world's leading technology companies.

Through Mindrift, over 10,000 professionals across 50+ domains and 15+ languages contribute to supervised fine-tuning, reinforcement learning from human feedback (RLHF), model evaluation, and red-teaming for frontier AI models developed by leading global technology companies.

Operated by Toloka from Amsterdam, Netherlands, Mindrift offers flexible, fully remote, project-based opportunities — empowering experts to shape the future of generative AI, one task at a time.

title: Marketing Intern
descriptionHtml:
🔥 WANTED: World Cup Content Maniac


SpotForFans.com — the fan HQ guide for FIFA World Cup 2026 — needs someone to blow up our TikTok and Instagram in the next 30 days.


This is not a normal marketing job. There is no brand guideline. There is no approval process. There is no "let's circle back on that."


There is: 11 US host cities. 78 matches. Millions of fans about to invade America. And zero people making content about where those fans should actually go.


That's where you come in, be bold, be creative, make a scene.


🎯 WHAT YOU'LL DO

• Run our TikTok and Instagram from zero to as big as you can get it before June 11

• Create 5-10 pieces of content per day — short-form video, carousels, reels, memes, chaos

• Start fights between cities (Miami vs Atlanta, LA vs Dallas — you pick the war)

• Start fights between fan bases (Brazil vs Argentina, Europe vs America — let them go)

• Make people argue in the comments. Make people tag their friends. Make people panic-plan their World Cup trip

• Turn our data (4,380+ bars across 11 cities, full match schedules) into content that hits


🧠 WHO YOU ARE

• You live on TikTok and Instagram. You know what's trending before it trends

• You've made something go viral before — or you're dying to

• You understand that controversy = engagement = growth

• You can make a video in 20 minutes with CapCut and free stock footage that looks like it took 2 hours

• You're a soccer fan, a sports fan, or at minimum you understand fan culture and tribal loyalty

• You don't need permission. You need wifi


💰 COMPENSATION

This is a revenue-share / equity role — no base salary. We're a pre-launch startup with near-zero costs and a massive time-sensitive opportunity. If you need a paycheck on the 1st, this isn't for you. If you want a piece of something that could pop off in the next 60 days, keep reading.


Compensation structure discussed with final candidates.


⚡ WHY THIS IS WORTH IT

• The World Cup is the biggest sporting event on Earth and it's happening HERE in 30 days

• There is no established competition — Eventbrite, Meetup, Yelp can't do what we do

• You get full creative control. No one is going to tell you to "tone it down"

• If you build the audience, you own that story forever — portfolio, career, clout

• If this takes off, you're a co-builder, not an employee


📍 DETAILS

• Remote (US-based preferred, any city)

• Start: immediately — every day counts

• Duration: World Cup window (now through July 2026)

• Time commitment: this is a full-send, not a side project


Apply with:

1. Your TikTok or IG handle (or any content you've made)

2. One idea for a SpotForFans video that would get 100K views

3. Which World Cup host city you'd roast first and why


No resume needed. Show us you get it.

  • spotforfans.com

companySlogan: See the future of entertainment with Skydeck Interactive Films.
companyDescription:
At Skydeck Interactive Films, we are committed to revolutionizing the way audiences experience storytelling. Our studio specializes in creating immersive and engaging interactive films that allow viewers to make choices and shape the outcome of the story. From interactive documentaries to narrative-driven dramas, we offer a range of services that blend the best of film and gaming to provide an unparalleled entertainment experience.

Our team of experienced storytellers, game designers, and VR/AR developers is dedicated to pushing the boundaries of what's possible in interactive media. We take pride in our ability to create compelling and thought-provoking stories that challenge conventional narratives and spark conversations.

At Skydeck, we believe that the future of entertainment is interactive, and we are excited to be at the forefront of this new era of storytelling. Contact us today to learn more about how we can bring your ideas to life and help you tell your story in a whole new way.

title: Senior Director, Head of Content - Panthers
descriptionHtml:
Position Overview

The Carolina Panthers are seeking a dynamic, positive leader to guide the strategy, tactical operations, and culture of the Club’s content team. This established leader of people will oversee the teams responsible for social media, video production, radio and television broadcast production, reporting, editorial, team website and app, and production operations. The ideal candidate will be adept at working with and leading both high-performing creatives and strategists, and will be prepared to guide the Club to innovate and lead the League in an evolving media landscape.

Primary Responsibilities

  • Lead the Content organization with the mindset of a Media Company GM – seeking to produce world-class content across platforms at a prolific and expanding rate with the aim to fuel a highly-engaged and consistently growing fanbase, while driving top-line revenue through smart and aligned corporate partnerships and media rights deals.
  • Set strategy and establish channel-right KPIs and benchmarks for all departments relating to content engagement, channel growth, brand goals, and revenue generation that streamline and align with department and individual goal setting.
  • Oversee all aspects of social media execution, web editorial direction, live streaming events, and content planning across O&O (web and app) platforms and emerging content distribution platforms.
  • Set yearly budgeting and spending for all teams and lead the development of strategic outside partnerships to help the team increase production capacities, capabilities, efficiencies, and alignment.
  • Work alongside Head of Creative to establish unified look, feel, and voice across properties that makes it clear and obvious when content is made by the Panthers, rather than about the Panthers. Establish the culture of a unified team across Content and Creative, with the Creative team providing graphic design, photography, special video projects, and motion graphics resources to fuel a prolific and unified Content output.
  • Work alongside Head of Creative with the relationship of an internal Creative Agency – drawing on the shared resource to support special event coverage, sponsorable content ideation and production, special project production, and other creative services.
  • Lead processes for effectively and efficiently staffing Content production roles across all team major events, especially games. Create systems to ensure our team can execute game coverage at a world-class level, while building efficiencies that streamline costs and a staffing rotation to create business continuity while providing the staff with work-life harmony.
  • Act as organizational contact for outside partners, including the NFL, product vendors, media companies, and others; be proactive in promoting the team’s brand and creative outputs wherever possible in the appropriate digital landscapes
  • Act as the Department’s lead contact with Football for day-to-day communications, approvals, and alignment. Maintain and further develop a culture of trust, aligned goals, and respect in order to maintain and advance access to protected football spaces for the benefit of our fans and the organization.
  • Work alongside Marketing department, Creative department, and others to drive a unified Integrated output around key NFL and Club tentpole moments. Align dedicated content verticals to support robust Content plans to support each key campaign, and align with Creative department to produce Special Projects to support overall campaign output while maintaining the day-to-day focus of the Content team.
  • Serve as liaison with NFL platform and content departments and as liaison with video and broadcast leads; manage talent and personnel contracts for preseason TV broadcasts and full-season radio broadcasts.
  • Manage senior-level direct reports, each responsible for managing others. Direct reports to include: Director, Social and Digital Strategy, Director, Digital Video, Executive Producer, Radio, and Sr. Manager, Broadcasting. Lead a full team of 27 with positive, accountable, selfless, and future-focused leadership. Direct-manage additional direct reports to backfill leadership roles whenever required.
  • Evaluate and lead individual growth of talent across departments, elevating performance and developing future leaders.

Minimum Qualifications

  • Bachelor’s degree in communications, marketing, advertising, or a related field
  • 10+ years of experience in content production, content strategy, media, or an adjacent space
  • Substantial professional experience leading a high-performing team of creative professionals, including both direct reports and a broader team.
  • Demonstrated experience leading a fully integrated digital and social program.
  • Strategic and commercial mindset with the ability to create and execute a proactive plan and pivot, as needed, to respond to unplanned needs.
  • Strong knowledge of best-in-class social and digital media practices, NFL narratives, and league storylines; experience optimizing social media platforms including Twitter, Facebook, Instagram, TikTok, LinkedIn, YouTube, and more.
  • Adept at working cross-functionally and providing exemplary service to internal and external stakeholders.
  • Must be able to work non-traditional hours, including nights, weekends and holidays when necessary. This role is expected to work every home game during the NFL season.
  • Must pass pre-employment screens
  • Must be able to handle confidential, privileged, and/or sensitive information carefully and with sensitivity

Skills For Success

  • Excellent oral, written, communication, and proofreading skills
  • Detail-oriented, must be able to multi-task, and must be able to manage time successfully
  • Ability to adapt to a fast-paced environment and think creatively
  • Ability to work on a team and independently

Physical Requirements

  • Sitting at a desk and computer for long periods of time.
  • Bending, standing, reaching, kneeling, and lifting technology equipment and office materials.
  • Walking throughout the stadium facility, sometimes for an extended period of time.

Work Environment

  • This job operates in a professional office environment (mostly office environment, outside in a stadium facility at times which may include inclement weather conditions).

The Carolina Panthers are an equal opportunity at-will employer and do not discriminate against any employee or applicant for employment because of age, race, religion, color, disability, sex, sexual orientation, or national origin.

companySlogan: Where Marketing Meets
companyDescription:
For the past 75 years, the American Marketing Association has been making an impact on the marketing world through resources and expertise provided to its members. In 1982, a chapter was established in Charlotte to help bring the innovation of the organization to a vastly growing region of the country. Since then the Charlotte American Marketing Association (CAMA) has become a vital element to the national organization and the Queen City!

Boasting a roster of over 200 members, CAMA is one of the largest professional organizations in the Queen City; offering networking opportunities and educational programs bringing awareness to current trends within the marketing, digital, and communications industries.

Over the past 30 years CAMA has helped enrich professionals'​ clout through its programming, network and knowledge. With a board and volunteers of 40+ marketeers, CAMA continues to provide an environment where marketers may connect, learn, and grow!

title: Polisher/Fabricator
descriptionHtml:
Description

COMPANY OVERVIEW

Accents & Interiors is a subsidiary of Artisan Design Group. Artisan Design Group (ADG), a Lowe’s company, is a leading national provider of interior finishings solutions for homebuilders and property management clients. ADG delivers end-to-end design, procurement and installation services for flooring, cabinets, countertops and a growing portfolio of interior products.

Founded in 2016, ADG has grown by uniting respected regional operators across the country. Following its 2025 acquisition by Lowe’s Companies, Inc., the company continues to operate as ADG while benefiting from the scale, resources and brand strength of one of the most trusted names in home improvement. Together, they are expanding their capabilities in a highly fragmented market while remaining committed to craftsmanship, service and local expertise, that make their teams valued partners nationwide.

Compensation Range

Pay Range: $21/hr. – $31/hr.

 Shift: Monday - Friday

Job Summary

Our growing company is currently in search for Fabricators/Polishers, Installers and Measures of Granite and Quartz countertops. The Polisher/Fabricator plays a critical role in the fabrication, installation, and measurement of granite and quartz countertops. This position works closely with internal teams, customers, and other departments to ensure projects are completed accurately, efficiently, and to the highest quality standards.

We are seeking hardworking, professional, and responsible individuals who are willing to follow detailed instructions, learn all aspects of the countertop fabrication and installation process, and develop the skills needed to perform each task proficiently once trained. Success in this role is demonstrated by the ability to complete countertop measurements and installations accurately, maintain quality craftsmanship, and contribute positively to team operations and customer satisfaction.

Requirements

ESSENTIAL DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES

  • Ability to work effectively as part of a team while demonstrating a willingness to learn all areas of the industry.
  • Strong attention to detail with the ability to follow instructions accurately.
  • Willingness and capability to learn how to safely operate power tools, including grinders and saws.
  • Valid driver’s license with a good driving record, where required for the position.
  • Knowledge of and adherence to Workplace Safety Data Sheets (WSDS) and safety standards.

Required Qualifications

EDUCATION, EXPERIENCE & QUALIFICATIONS

  • High School Diploma or GED
  • WSDL experience
  • Self-Sufficient
  • Basic math skills
  • Basic analytical skills
  • Strong communication skills, both verbal and written
  • Valid Driver’s License

Preferred Qualifications

  • 2-5 years of experience for templating natural stone and Quartz is helpful but not required
  • Bilingual

PHYSICAL DEMANDS

The physical demands described below are representative of those required to successfully perform the essential functions of this position.

  • Lifting/Carrying: Frequently lifts and/or moves items weighing up to 50 pounds.
  • Mobility: Frequently required to move throughout the warehouse, or job site to access materials and equipment.
  • Positioning: May be required to position oneself to inspect, maintain, or install materials, including stooping, kneeling, crouching, or crawling.

WORK ENVIRONMENT

The work environment characteristics described below are representative of those encountered while performing the essential functions of this position.

  • Warehouse/Facility: May be exposed to non-climate-controlled environments (heat and cold), moving mechanical parts, vibration, and elevated noise levels.
  • Job Site/Field: Exposure to active construction sites, outdoor weather conditions, dust, and fumes. Requires use of appropriate Personal Protective Equipment (PPE), such as hard hats, safety glasses, and steel-toed boots.
  • Travel: Requires travel to local job sites.

Our Benefits

  • Health Insurance (Medical, Prescription, Dental, and Vision)
  • Life Insurance
  • Paid Holidays and Time Off
  • 401(k) Plan with company matching

ADG is an equal opportunity employer and administers all personnel practices without regard to race, color, religious creed, sex, gender, age, ancestry, national origin, mental or physical disability or medical condition, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, marital status, military or veteran status, genetic information, or any other category protected under federal, state, or local law.

We are committed to helping individuals with disabilities participate in the workforce and ensuring equal opportunity to apply and compete for jobs. If you need any assistance at any point in the application or hiring process due to a disability and you need an accommodation, please email accommodations@adgus.net. Please do not use this email address for any other questions. Only inquiries regarding accommodations will be addressed.

Our growing company is currently in search for Fabricators/Polishers, Installers and Measures of Granite and Quartz countertops. The Polisher/Fabricator plays a critical role in the fabrication, installation, and measurement of granite and quartz countertops. This position works closely with internal teams, customers, and other departments to ensure projects are completed accurately, efficiently, and to the highest quality standards.

We are seeking hardworking, professional, and responsible individuals who are willing to follow detailed instructions, learn all aspects of the countertop fabrication and installation process, and develop the skills needed to perform each task proficiently once trained. Success in this role is demonstrated by the ability to complete countertop measurements and installations accurately, maintain quality craftsmanship, and contribute positively to team operations and customer satisfaction.

Requirements

ESSENTIAL DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES 

Ability to work effectively as part of a team while demonstrating a willingness to learn all areas of the industry.Strong attention to detail with the ability to follow instructions accurately.Willingness and capability to learn how to safely operate power tools, including grinders and saws.Valid driver’s license with a good driving record, where required for the position.Knowledge of and adherence to Workplace Safety Data Sheets (WSDS) and safety standards.

Required Qualifications

EDUCATION, EXPERIENCE & QUALIFICATIONS 

High School Diploma or GEDWSDL experienceSelf-SufficientBasic math skillsBasic analytical skillsStrong communication skills, both verbal and writtenValid Driver’s License

Preferred Qualifications

2-5 years of experience for templating natural stone and Quartz is helpful but not requiredBilingual 

PHYSICAL DEMANDS

The physical demands described below are representative of those required to successfully perform the essential functions of this position.

Lifting/Carrying: Frequently lifts and/or moves items weighing up to 50 pounds.Mobility: Frequently required to move throughout the warehouse, or job site to access materials and equipment.Positioning: May be required to position oneself to inspect, maintain, or install materials, including stooping, kneeling, crouching, or crawling.

WORK ENVIRONMENT

The work environment characteristics described below are representative of those encountered while performing the essential functions of this position.

 Warehouse/Facility: May be exposed to non-climate-controlled environments (heat and cold), moving mechanical parts, vibration, and elevated noise levels. Job Site/Field: Exposure to active construction sites, outdoor weather conditions, dust, and fumes. Requires use of appropriate Personal Protective Equipment (PPE), such as hard hats, safety glasses, and steel-toed boots. Travel: Requires travel to local job sites.

Our Benefits

Health Insurance (Medical, Prescription, Dental, and Vision)Life InsurancePaid Holidays and Time Off401(k) Plan with company matching

ADG is an equal opportunity employer and administers all personnel practices without regard to race, color, religious creed, sex, gender, age, ancestry, national origin, mental or physical disability or medical condition, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, marital status, military or veteran status, genetic information, or any other category protected under federal, state, or local law.

We are committed to helping individuals with disabilities participate in the workforce and ensuring equal opportunity to apply and compete for jobs. If you need any assistance at any point in the application or hiring process due to a disability and you need an accommodation, please email accommodations@adgus.net. Please do not use this email address for any other questions. Only inquiries regarding accommodations will be addressed.

Everyone at Accents & Interiors is committed to helping our customers help their customers realize their vision for their home. We trust you’ll find our service exceeds your expectations.

title: Program Manager (III) [210071]
descriptionHtml:
Aquent is proud to partner with a leading global technology company at the forefront of innovation. This organization is dedicated to solving complex challenges and building the future through cutting-edge solutions that impact millions of users worldwide.

Are you a visionary leader with a passion for driving impactful programs? We are seeking a highly skilled Program Manager to join our client’s dynamic team and spearhead critical initiatives that transcend organizational boundaries. In this pivotal role, you won’t just manage projects; you’ll shape strategic direction, foster collaboration, and deliver transformative improvements that directly influence business outcomes and user experiences. You will be instrumental in navigating complex, cross-functional challenges, ensuring strategic alignment, and delivering tangible results that drive the organization forward.

What You’ll Do

  • Independently define, drive, and own comprehensive program charters, addressing scope, timelines, objectives, resourcing, risks, and dependencies for moderately complex programs across multiple teams.
  • Translate ambiguous program goals into clear, measurable objectives and milestones, determining the most effective level of program management required for each initiative.
  • Develop detailed project plans that break down initiatives into discrete phases, proactively identifying risks, dependencies, and potential constraints.
  • Independently establish program phasing and metrics to ensure continuous improvement and optimal resource stewardship.
  • Design and deliver optimized communication strategies, including executive and key partner communications, effectively representing the program across all touchpoints.
  • Anticipate and proactively manage change management requirements, developing rollout plans, monitoring progress, and resolving misalignments to ensure smooth transitions.
  • Cultivate trusted relationships with senior program stakeholders, facilitating effective decision-making, resolving prioritization conflicts, and proactively managing escalations.
  • Independently determine and collect essential data for program governance, maintaining project trackers and status reports to inform stakeholder decisions.
  • Execute program tasks, proactively manage dependencies, and integrate values of excellence, inclusion, trust, and reputation into all aspects of program delivery.
  • Develop automated processes to enhance program velocity and monitoring, and define robust program governance structures, including mitigation strategies, postmortems, and escalation reporting.
  • Drive effective and productive program stakeholder meetings.

Must-Have Qualifications

  • Proficiency in Portfolio Management
  • Demonstrated expertise in Team Management
  • Strong background in Continuous Improvement methodologies
  • Experience in Managing Organizational Change
  • Exceptional Communications Management skills
  • Advanced Planning capabilities
  • Proven ability in Stakeholder Management
  • Expertise in Execution and Governance
  • Sound Technical Judgment
  • Deep User understanding and empathy

About Aquent Talent

Aquent Talent connects the best talent in marketing, creative, and design with the world’s biggest brands.

Our eligible talent get access to amazing benefits like subsidized health, vision, and dental plans, paid sick leave, and retirement plans with a match. We also offer free online training through Aquent Gymnasium. More information on our awesome benefits!

Aquent is an equal-opportunity employer. We evaluate qualified applicants without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability, veteran status, and other legally protected characteristics. We’re about creating an inclusive environment-one where different backgrounds, experiences, and perspectives are valued, and everyone can contribute, grow their careers, and thrive.

Client Description

Join one of the world’s most iconic technology visionaries, consistently recognized as a global leader in digital innovation. Our client offers a high-energy, collaborative environment where you will work alongside some of the industry’s brightest minds. This is a unique opportunity to gain experience within a multi-billion dollar tech giant, contributing to high-visibility projects that influence how the world connects and learns. For professionals looking to elevate their career, there is no better place to build your portfolio and witness large-scale innovation in action.

title: Sales Manager
descriptionHtml:
Job Description

Title: Sales Manager

Pioneer the next generation of innovation. Join us and you’ll develop your skills and expertise to the very highest levels, working in an international environment for a company known the world over for its brilliance.

Key Accountabilities:


  • Manage after sales business opportunities for Direct End Customers and Distributors/Dealers supporting the Stationary Business Unit, specific to Natural Gas Power Generation
  • Provide responsive assistance to coordinate and support Direct End Customers and Distributors/Dealers on requests for call out services, product information and quality concerns specific to Natural Gas Power Generation
  • Quote Direct End Customers and Distributors/Dealers in prospecting for new sales representing the Natural Gas Power Generation after sales product line
  • Promote Direct End Customer and Distributor/Dealer training with regards to after sales parts topics, systems, products, programs and promotions.
  • Ensure that parts inventory and training levels are sufficient (ex. at Distributors/Dealers) to provide quality sales and after sales support to Natural Gas Power Generation customers
  • Support new business opportunities for mtu after sales products, develop and execute proposals, quotes and follow through to make the sale
  • Collect forecasts and develop business cases for future mtu after sales products and services
  • Track and report on industry trends, business conditions and account status to develop future products and sales
  • Identify and resolve customer concerns and complaints in a timely and satisfactory manner
  • Monitor, implement and evaluate mtu's procedures, policies and agreements to increase customer awareness and compliance
  • Prepare and present professional presentations to customers, management and staff
  • Maintain knowledge of mtu products, applications, after sales systems, procedures and policies
  • Attend trade shows and events and reports activities
  • Promote a compliance culture in area of responsibility and live the letter and the spirit of the Rolls-Royce Code of Conduct.
  • Perform special projects as required

Basic Requirements:


  • Bachelor’s degree in Engineering or Business Management and 3 years of applicable experience in support, sales or parts-marketing within after-sales; or 6 years of experience in support, sales or parts marketing within after sales
  • Must be able to speak and communicate fluently in Spanish
  • To be considered for this role you must be a US Citizen or Green Card Holder

Preferred Qualifications:


  • Strong ability to be a self-starter and work extended periods without supervision
  • Strong oral and written communication skills
  • Strong interpersonal skills
  • Strong organizational, planning and follow-up skills
  • Strong analytical ability to handle complex issues
  • Strong problem-solving skills and the ability to make recommendations
  • Project management skills
  • Ability and willingness to travel domestic and international on short notice, up to 50% of the time
  • Proficient with PC and MS Office Suite
  • Knowledge of MTU organization (products, policies & procedures)
  • Adequate knowledge of heavy-duty diesel/gas engines or comparable diesel/gas products and/or Generator Sets
  • Knowledge of SAP operating system

Rolls-Royce is an Equal Opportunity Employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to any protected characteristics.

At Rolls-Royce, we are committed to creating a workplace where all employees feel respected, supported, and empowered to do their best work. We foster a welcoming and innovative work environment that invests in you, giving you access to an incredible breadth and depth of opportunities where you can grow your career and make a difference.

Job Posting Date

14 May 2026; 00:05

Pay Range

$103,576 - $168,311-Annually

Location:

Novi

Benefits

Rolls-Royce provides a comprehensive and competitive Total Rewards package that includes base pay and a discretionary bonus plan. Eligible employees may have the opportunity to enroll in other benefits, including health, dental, vision, disability, life and accidental death & dismemberment insurance; a flexible spending account; a health savings account; a 401(k) retirement savings plan with a company match; Employee Assistance Program; Paid Time Off; certain paid holidays; paid parental and family care leave; tuition reimbursement; and a long-term incentive plan. The options available to an employee may vary depending on eligibility factors such as date of hire, and employment type.

The Business Unit Power Systems of Rolls-Royce provides world-class power solutions and complete life-cycle support under our product and solution brand mtu. Through digitalization and electrification, we strive to develop drive and power generation solutions that are even cleaner and smarter and thus provide answers to the challenges posed by the rapidly growing societal demands for energy and mobility.

We deliver and service comprehensive, powerful and reliable systems, based on both gas and diesel engines, as well as electrified hybrid systems. These clean and technologically-advanced solutions serve our customers in the marine and infrastructure sectors worldwide.

companyDescription:
A warm welcome to the official Linkedin page of Rolls-Royce Power Systems. This is where you’ll find the latest news and insights on our company. We look forward to receiving your comments, questions and likes. For any Sales & Service questions please contact our Customer Assistance Center around the clock, 365 days a year: info@cac-ps.rolls-royce.com or https://www.mtu-solutions.com/eu/en/contact/customer-assistance-center.html 

The Power Systems Business Unit of Rolls-Royce is focused on creating sustainable, climate neutral solutions for drive, propulsion and power generation. We are making a significant contribution to the energy transition with environmentally-friendly technologies from our mtu product and solution brand. As leaders in standby power for safety-critical plants and in integrated drive and propulsion systems for ships and heavy-duty land vehicles, our customers know they can depend on us, and have been doing so for over 110 years.
Rolls-Royce Power Systems is headquartered in Friedrichshafen in southern Germany and employs around 11,000 people. The product portfolio includes mtu-brand high-speed engines and propulsion systems for ships, power generation, heavy land, rail and defence vehicles and for the oil and gas industry as well as diesel and gas systems and battery containers for mission critical, standby and continuous power, combined generation of heat and power, and microgrids and is intensively engaged in the development of climate-neutral solutions.

title: Research Assistant - Part Time
descriptionHtml:
Orchestrate Consulting's mission is to help job seekers access carefully coordinated opportunities that match their career goals. We are not a staffing firm or agency. Orchestrate Consulting does not hire for these roles—we curate and validate them from verified employers.

Employer Industry: Clinical Research

Why Consider This Job Opportunity

  • Salary up to $30,000 annually
  • Opportunity for flexible part-time work at 20 hours a week
  • Supportive and collaborative work environment
  • Engage in meaningful work within the clinical research field
  • Comprehensive benefits package including medical benefits, PTO, and holiday pay

What To Expect (Job Responsibilities)

  • Support project teams in the completion of commercial and government clinical research projects
  • Create, review, and maintain project documentation according to project requirements
  • Process incoming and outgoing project materials to ensure appropriate distribution
  • Interact with clinical site staff regarding study activities, questions, and problems
  • Prepare general correspondence, reports, and other project-specific documentation

What Is Required (Qualifications)

  • Bachelor's Degree in science, health, or a related field
  • Knowledge of Microsoft Office tools such as Word, Excel, and PowerPoint
  • Must be comfortable working in a fast-paced environment with multiple projects
  • Exceptional interpersonal, written, and verbal communication skills
  • Strong organizational skills; accurate and detail-oriented

How To Stand Out (Preferred Qualifications)

  • Experience in clinical research methodologies
  • Familiarity with project management tools and techniques
  • Previous experience in a research or clinical setting

title: Allocation Analyst- The Disney Store
descriptionHtml:
About The Role & Team

At Disney Consumer Products, we inspire imagination around the world and are committed to creating happiness for families and fans by bringing captivating, inspiring, and inclusive products into their daily lives. From toys to t-shirts, console games, books, and more, our team brings our beloved brands and franchises into the lives of families through products and experiences that can be found worldwide, such as the Disney Store e-commerce platform, Disney Parks, local and international retailers, and Disney Store locations around the world.

As a Disney Allocation Analyst, you will be responsible for performing routine assignments by using existing procedures to deliver against weekly inventory allocation plans for your assigned department(s), considering business strategies, sales targets and pricing activity to support the Disney Store omni-channel business.

This is a full-time role

You will report to the Manager, Retail Allocation

What You Will Do:


  • Maintain forward looking weekly inventory flow forecast and review with manager, cross functional partners, and broader Allocation team on a weekly basis
  • Set up and maintain stage set recap tool to confirm initial inventory plans, and align on final item level strategies with cross functional partners
  • Execute initial stage set allocations to support planned inventory placement by store/channel to meet stage set/launch dates
  • Analyze data daily/weekly and propose ongoing replenishment allocations to maintain appropriate inventory levels by selling channel throughout product lifecycle to maximize profitability and minimize imbalances
  • Maintain various department tools/reporting to support inventory decisions/activities
  • Participate in weekly Allocation and cross functional team meetings to discuss inventory strategies and identify/resolve issues as necessary
  • Collaborate within the Allocation department to drive efficiencies and best practices
  • Additional responsibilities related to role as business needs require


Required Qualifications & Skills:


  • 1+ years of experience in inventory management, retail planning, or related role
  • Knowledge with specialty retail and/or E-commerce preferred
  • Detail oriented with strong organizational skill and ability to prioritize tasks effectively
  • Strong communication skills and ability to effectively work within a cross-functional team
  • High level of computer literacy with a focus on Excel
  • Exposure to relevant analytical and data management systems and reporting types a plus
  • Positive attitude, high motivation, curiosity, and willingness to learn


Required Education


  • Bachelor's Degree or equivalent experience


Additional Information

Disney offers a rewards package to help you live your best life. This includes health and savings benefits, educational opportunities, and special extras that only Disney can provide. Learn more about our benefits and perks at https://jobs.disneycareers.com/benefits.

#DXMedia

#DCPJobs

The hiring range for this position in Glendale, CA is $59,000 to $79,800 per year. The base pay actually offered will take into account internal equity and also may vary depending on the candidate’s geographic region, job-related knowledge, skills, and experience among other factors. A bonus and/or long-term incentive units may be provided as part of the compensation package, in addition to the full range of medical, financial, and/or other benefits, dependent on the level and position offered.

Disney Experiences brings the magic of Disney stories and franchises to life through theme parks, resorts, cruise ships, unique vacation experiences, products and more around the world.

Parks
Disney Parks is one of the world’s leading providers of family travel and leisure experiences, with six resort destinations across the globe, including 12 theme parks, 55 resort hotels, and other unique vacation experiences.

Experiences
Disney Signature Experiences offers family travel and leisure experiences beyond theme parks inclusive of Disney Cruise Line, Disney Vacation Club, Aulani, A Disney Resort & Spa, Adventures By Disney, National Geographic Expeditions and Storyliving by Disney.

Products
Disney Consumer Products brings beloved brands and franchises into the daily lives of families and fans through products – from toys to t-shirts, apps, books, console games and more – on the Disney Store e-commerce platform and at Disney Parks, local and international retailers, as well as Disney Store locations globally.

title: Freelance Junior Journalist - AI Trainer
descriptionHtml:
Please submit your resume in English and indicate your level of English proficiency.

Mindrift connects specialists with project-based AI opportunities for leading tech companies, focused on testing, evaluating, and improving AI systems. Participation isproject-based, not permanent employment.

What This Opportunity Involves

As an AI Trainer - Junior Journalist, your work will help train AI models, shaping how they understand and generate human-like text. This isn't just traditional writing—you'll be crafting text that teaches AI reasoning, logic accuracy, nuance, and clarity.

While each project involves unique tasks, contributors may:

  • Craft original, clear, and fact-checked responses based on project guidelines.
  • Generate prompts that challenge AI.
  • Define comprehensive scoring criteria to evaluate the accuracy of the AI's answers.
  • Follow style and quality standards to ensure consistency

What We Look For

This opportunity is a good fit if you are seeking for open to part-time, non-permanent projects. Ideally, contributors will have:

  • Bachelor's degree in Journalism, Communications, Linguistics, Literature, or Education to ensure a strong understanding of grammar and stylistic features;
  • At least 1 year of professional experience in Journalism, Communications, PR, etc., with strong skills in critical thinking and working with text in English language;
  • Strong written English (C1/C2);
  • Stable internet connection.

How It Works

Apply → Pass qualification(s) → Join a project → Complete tasks → Get paid

Project time expectations

For this project, tasks are estimated to require around 10-20 hours per week during active phases, based on project requirements. This is an estimate, not a guaranteed workload, and applies only while the project is active.

Compensation

On this project, contributors can earn up to $30 per hour equivalent, depending on their level and pace of contribution.

Compensation varies across projects depending on scope, complexity, and required expertise. Please note that other projects on the platform may offer different earning levels based on their requirements.

title: Operations Manager (III) [209355]
descriptionHtml:
A leading global company, renowned for innovation and transforming industries, is seeking exceptional talent. Partnering with Aquent, we are looking for a visionary professional to join their dynamic team.

Imagine being at the forefront of operational excellence, where your strategic vision directly shapes how a global leader designs, optimizes, and scales its most critical processes. This isn’t just a job; it’s an opportunity to redefine efficiency, champion innovation, and make a tangible impact on large-scale operations that touch millions. You’ll be the architect of seamless workflows, the driver of continuous improvement, and a key player in ensuring operational success across diverse teams and initiatives.

Responsibilities

  • 5+ years leading the design, launch, standardization, and optimization of operational processes for both vendor and internal teams.
  • Leverage comprehensive knowledge of process optimization to implement solutions such as re-engineering, automation, outsourcing, and consolidation of service lines.
  • Oversee the creation and maintenance of Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs), process maps, workflow documentation, help resources, and relevant training and certification programs.
  • Contribute significantly to the execution of projects aimed at solving scaled operations problems, partnering with relevant teams on scoping, execution, and management.
  • Conduct thorough tracking and analysis of operational trends in process or workflow performance to identify and implement improvements, manage utilization, eliminate non-productive activities, and foster cross-site efficiencies.
  • Influence automation and robotics enhancements to further optimize operational procedures.
  • Manage supplier and internal selection processes, onboarding, performance reviews, and progress evaluations for specific projects, integrating them into overarching project strategies.
  • Define and identify proper metrics, ensure the use of accurate data, and optimize data display in dashboards, developing and communicating operational metrics/KPIs.
  • Provide critical feedback to service providers and internal stakeholders on process and product improvements to simplify complex workflows and scale operations.
  • Collaborate with partner teams to set and implement process improvements and changes, defining and implementing workflow efficiency scoring frameworks to proactively identify execution gaps.
  • Champion the customer experience for end-to-end programs, policies, and operations.
  • Ensure operations comply with legal, risk, financial, and privacy guidelines, providing operational leadership to workflows and engaging service provider teams for effective internal and external operations.
  • Develop robust operations review processes to gain insights into performance and proactively identify solutions.
  • Strategize with quality specialists or suppliers to ensure adherence to SLAs and PRDs, delivering business reviews that summarize performance and facilitate feedback on scaling strategies.
  • Oversee question resolution within SLA, manage employee query exceptions and escalations, and streamline high-volume ticket types.
  • Cultivate strong relationships with stakeholders, helping them understand policies and processes, and assisting with escalations and exceptions.
  • Share and scale successes, improvement opportunities, best practices, and learnings both internally and externally.
  • Facilitate meetings with stakeholders to identify business objectives and operational requirements, contributing to the delivery of business requirements with optimal internal and external strategies (e.g., right partner, location, cost, budget).
  • Lead the ideation, launch, and scaling of solutions that enhance operational efficiency, customer experience, and key success metrics (e.g., cost savings, capacity planning, time savings) at various organizational levels.
  • Manage strategic relationships with key stakeholders to deliver and manage operations and processes effectively.

Qualifications

  • Proficiency in data analysis, synthesis, and planning.
  • Deep understanding of operational Service Level Agreements (SLAs).
  • Expertise in queue and ticket management.
  • Demonstrated team management capabilities.
  • Strong customer support skills.
  • Familiarity with automation tools and processes.
  • Proven stakeholder relationship management abilities.
  • Solid client and domain knowledge.
  • A track record of identifying automation opportunities.
  • Exceptional project management skills.
  • Relevant industry knowledge.
  • Comprehensive business operations knowledge.
  • Proficiency in client/partner management.
  • Strong consultative skills.
  • Expertise in process design and improvement.
  • Effective negotiation skills.
  • Acute business acumen.

About Aquent Talent

Aquent Talent connects the best talent in marketing, creative, and design with the world’s biggest brands.

Our eligible talent get access to amazing benefits like subsidized health, vision, and dental plans, paid sick leave, and retirement plans with a match. We also offer free online training through Aquent Gymnasium. More information on our awesome benefits!

Aquent is an equal-opportunity employer. We evaluate qualified applicants without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability, veteran status, and other legally protected characteristics. We’re about creating an inclusive environment-one where different backgrounds, experiences, and perspectives are valued, and everyone can contribute, grow their careers, and thrive.

Client Description

Join one of the world’s most iconic technology visionaries, consistently recognized as a global leader in digital innovation. Our client offers a high-energy, collaborative environment where you will work alongside some of the industry’s brightest minds. This is a unique opportunity to gain experience within a multi-billion dollar tech giant, contributing to high-visibility projects that influence how the world connects and learns. For professionals looking to elevate their career, there is no better place to build your portfolio and witness large-scale innovation in action.

companySlogan: We help guide businesses toward the talent, technology, and services they need to create a better future of work.
companyDescription: Aquent is the leading global work solutions company helping guide businesses toward the talent, technology, and services they need to excel. From specialty recruiting and talent experience to creative strategy and project management, we’re making the future of work better for everyone. title: Material Manager
descriptionHtml:
Qualification

Masters / MSc / MBA (Operations/Finance) / MCom / BCom

Hospital Administration / Supply Chain Management or any related Master’s Degree

Department

Administration

Experience Required

Freshers can apply

Job Description

The Material Manager will be responsible for efficient procurement, storage, inventory control, and distribution of materials required for hospital operations. The role ensures uninterrupted availability of medical and non-medical supplies while maintaining cost efficiency, compliance with hospital policies, and proper coordination with all departments.

Key Responsibilities

  • Oversee procurement and inventory management of hospital supplies
  • Ensure timely availability of materials across departments
  • Maintain vendor coordination and supply chain efficiency
  • Monitor stock levels and reduce wastage/cost inefficiencies
  • Ensure compliance with hospital policies and procedures

Maximum Age

35

companyDescription:
Cachar Cancer Hospital & Research Centre is administered by the Cachar Cancer Hospital Society. It is situated in Silchar,  Assam, India. The hospital provides free and subsidized treatment  to the poor and underserved patients in Assam and other neighbouring states like Arunachal Pradesh, Manipur, Mizoram,Meghalaya, Tripura and  Nagaland,.Dr. Ravi Kannan is the Director of the Hospital and you can reach him at ravi.kannan@cacharcancerhospital.org

Through this page we hope to connect with Corporates with Healthcare as their CSR,individuals who would like to donate to our cause, other grant organizations and other cancer groups.

title: Vice President Product Management
descriptionHtml:
Acuity Inc. (NYSE: AYI) is a market-leading industrial technology company. We use technology to solve problems in spaces, light and more things to come. Through our two business segments, Acuity Brands Lighting (ABL) and Acuity Intelligent Spaces (AIS), we design, manufacture, and bring to market products and services that make a valuable difference in people’s lives.

We achieve growth through the development of innovative new products and services, including lighting, lighting controls, building management solutions, and an audio, video and control platform. We focus on customer outcomes and drive growth and productivity to increase market share and deliver superior returns. We look to aggressively deploy capital to grow the business and to enter attractive new verticals.

Acuity Inc. is based in Atlanta, Georgia, with operations across North America, Europe and Asia. The Company is powered by approximately 13,000 dedicated and talented associates. Visit us at www.acuityinc.com.

Work location:

  • This position may be based anywhere in the United States and includes travel as part of the responsibilities.

Job Summary

The role sits at the intersection of Product Management, Engineering Leadership, and Enterprise Technology, accountable for outcomes across platform strategy, delivery performance, and adoption. It is foundational to Acuity’s ability to scale engineered-product innovation, accelerate time-to-market, improve quality, and increase reuse across the value chain. The ideal candidate is a product-first platform leader who can lead engineering organizations and drive AI-enabled SDLC and broader AI adoption with strong governance and measurable value.

Key Tasks & Responsibilities (Essential Functions)

Product Vision, Platform Strategy & Business Partnership

  • Act as a strategic partner to Product Engineering, Product Management, Manufacturing, and Sourcing leaders to define the target-state ecosystem and measurable outcomes.
  • Own a multi-year platform vision and portfolio strategy spanning product data, engineering enablement, integration patterns, and shared services that improve speed, quality, cost, and scalability.
  • Establish and evolve the product and engineering operating model (decision rights, intake, prioritization, architecture governance, delivery cadence, and value measurement) across cross-functional stakeholders.
  • Drive AI-enabled SDLC adoption (e.g., copilots, automated testing, code review assistance, documentation, and release automation) with responsible AI guardrails and clear productivity/quality KPIs.
  • Translate enterprise priorities into an outcome-driven roadmap with dependency sequencing, funding strategy, and realized value tracking—ensuring platforms operate as products, not projects.

Product Data, Digital Thread & Engineering Workflow Enablement

  • Own the enterprise strategy for product data interoperability across authoring tools, product data platforms, and downstream systems—ensuring consistent semantics, versioning, and traceability.
  • Partner with engineering and product leaders to enable scalable workflows for authoring, review, change, reuse, and collaboration—backed by clear standards for structures, attributes, classification, and validation.
  • Advance digital thread capabilities that connect design intent, engineering decisions, manufacturing readiness, and commercialization—improving cycle time, quality, and compliance.
  • Apply AI/automation to improve product data quality and engineering throughput (e.g., attribute suggestions, component matching, document generation, change impact analysis, and knowledge capture).

Manufacturing, Sourcing & Downstream Enablement

  • Ensure product definitions and changes flow reliably across Manufacturing, Sourcing, Quality, ERP, CPQ, and Digital Commerce—establishing authoritative sources, stewardship, and integration contracts.
  • Reduce downstream friction by standardizing release readiness, change propagation, and compliance processes—supported by automation and clear SLAs.
  • Partner with manufacturing and GTM leaders to ensure structures, attributes, and configuration rules are execution-ready, enabling accurate build, price, configure, and fulfill.
  • Drive a single, scalable model for item and product master data, configuration logic, and impact analysis across engineering and GTM systems.

Other

Product Management, Technology & Organizational Excellence

  • Build and lead a high-impact product & platform organization spanning product management, platform engineering, architecture, and delivery leadership.
  • Set standards for product discovery, problem framing, roadmap hygiene, prioritization, and outcome measurement—paired with strong engineering practices for execution.
  • Own the engineering effectiveness agenda: developer experience, CI/CD maturity, test automation, reliability/observability, and reduction of technical debt.
  • Partner with Engineering and Enterprise Architecture to manage modernization, scalability, security, resiliency, and platform governance.
  • Champion AI adoption across the product engineering value stream (SDLC and business workflows), including enablement, change management, responsible AI controls, and measurable business outcomes.

Executive Leadership & Influence

  • Own portfolio-level investment strategy and prioritization, including multi-year funding models, business cases, and ROI tracking.
  • Operate as a trusted peer to senior Engineering, Manufacturing, Sourcing, Product, and Technology leaders, co-owning outcomes rather than serving as a delivery function.
  • Communicate clearly and credibly at the executive level on strategy, roadmap progress, risks, dependencies, and realized enterprise value.
  • Represent the Product Engineering platform strategy in enterprise governance, planning, and transformation forums.

Skills And Minimum Experience Required

  • 15+ years of progressive leadership across product management, engineering/platform leadership, and enterprise technology.
  • Proven ability to define product vision and translate it into roadmaps, operating models, and measurable outcomes across cross-functional stakeholders.
  • Demonstrated experience leading engineering organizations through modern SDLC transformation (CI/CD, automated testing, DevSecOps, reliability/observability) and improving delivery performance.
  • Strong fluency in platform, data, and integration architectures, with the ability to balance speed, quality, risk, and scalability.
  • Executive presence with the ability to influence across business and technology domains and drive adoption at scale.

Preferred Skills And Experience

  • Experience building and scaling platform product teams (product + engineering) and driving durable adoption across diverse user communities.
  • Hands-on experience applying AI/ML and automation to the SDLC and enterprise workflows (e.g., copilots, test generation, intelligent triage, knowledge management) with responsible AI practices.
  • Familiarity with product lifecycle/product data domains (e.g., PLM/PDM, BOM/change workflows, CAD integrations, PIM/MDM) and the broader engineered-product value stream.
  • Experience integrating product and engineering platforms with ERP, CPQ, Digital Commerce, and MDM ecosystems.
  • MBA or equivalent business leadership experience.
  • Systems thinker who balances long-term platform strategy with engineering and operational realities; comfortable operating in ambiguity and simplifying complex domains.

Travel Requirements

  • 21-40%

The range for this position is $201,000 to $402,000. Placement within this range may vary, depending on the applicant’s experience and geographic location. Acuity offers generous benefits including health care, dental coverage, vision plans, 401K benefits, and commissions/incentive compensation depending on the role. For a list of our benefits, click here.

We value diversity and are an equal opportunity employer.  All qualified applicants will be considered for employment without regards to race, color, age, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity and expression, ethnicity or national origin, disability, pregnancy, religion, covered veteran status, protected genetic information, or any other characteristic protected by law. 

Please click here and here for more information.

If you have difficulty using our online system due to a disability and need an accommodation, you may contact us at (770) 922-9000, select option 4.  Please clearly indicate what type of accommodation you are requesting and for what requisition. 

 

Any unsolicited resumes sent to Acuity Inc. from a third party, such as an Agency recruiter, including unsolicited resumes sent to an Acuity Inc. mailing address, fax machine or email address, directly to Acuity Inc. employees, or to Acuity Inc. resume database will be considered Acuity Inc. property. Acuity Inc. will NOT pay a fee for any placement resulting from the receipt of an unsolicited resume.
