descriptionText:
Acuity Inc. (NYSE: AYI) is a market-leading industrial technology company. We use technology to solve problems in spaces, light and more things to come. Through our two business segments, Acuity Brands Lighting (ABL) and Acuity Intelligent Spaces (AIS), we design, manufacture, and bring to market products and services that make a valuable difference in people’s lives.

We achieve growth through the development of innovative new products and services, including lighting, lighting controls, building management solutions, and an audio, video and control platform. We focus on customer outcomes and drive growth and productivity to increase market share and deliver superior returns. We look to aggressively deploy capital to grow the business and to enter attractive new verticals.

Acuity Inc. is based in Atlanta, Georgia, with operations across North America, Europe and Asia. The Company is powered by approximately 13,000 dedicated and talented associates. Visit us at www.acuityinc.com.

Work location:

This position may be based anywhere in the United States and includes travel as part of the responsibilities.

Job Summary

The role sits at the intersection of Product Management, Engineering Leadership, and Enterprise Technology, accountable for outcomes across platform strategy, delivery performance, and adoption. It is foundational to Acuity’s ability to scale engineered-product innovation, accelerate time-to-market, improve quality, and increase reuse across the value chain. The ideal candidate is a product-first platform leader who can lead engineering organizations and drive AI-enabled SDLC and broader AI adoption with strong governance and measurable value.

Key Tasks & Responsibilities (Essential Functions)

Product Vision, Platform Strategy & Business Partnership

Act as a strategic partner to Product Engineering, Product Management, Manufacturing, and Sourcing leaders to define the target-state ecosystem and measurable outcomes.Own a multi-year platform vision and portfolio strategy spanning product data, engineering enablement, integration patterns, and shared services that improve speed, quality, cost, and scalability.Establish and evolve the product and engineering operating model (decision rights, intake, prioritization, architecture governance, delivery cadence, and value measurement) across cross-functional stakeholders.Drive AI-enabled SDLC adoption (e.g., copilots, automated testing, code review assistance, documentation, and release automation) with responsible AI guardrails and clear productivity/quality KPIs.Translate enterprise priorities into an outcome-driven roadmap with dependency sequencing, funding strategy, and realized value tracking—ensuring platforms operate as products, not projects.

Product Data, Digital Thread & Engineering Workflow Enablement

Own the enterprise strategy for product data interoperability across authoring tools, product data platforms, and downstream systems—ensuring consistent semantics, versioning, and traceability.Partner with engineering and product leaders to enable scalable workflows for authoring, review, change, reuse, and collaboration—backed by clear standards for structures, attributes, classification, and validation.Advance digital thread capabilities that connect design intent, engineering decisions, manufacturing readiness, and commercialization—improving cycle time, quality, and compliance.Apply AI/automation to improve product data quality and engineering throughput (e.g., attribute suggestions, component matching, document generation, change impact analysis, and knowledge capture).

Manufacturing, Sourcing & Downstream Enablement

Ensure product definitions and changes flow reliably across Manufacturing, Sourcing, Quality, ERP, CPQ, and Digital Commerce—establishing authoritative sources, stewardship, and integration contracts.Reduce downstream friction by standardizing release readiness, change propagation, and compliance processes—supported by automation and clear SLAs.Partner with manufacturing and GTM leaders to ensure structures, attributes, and configuration rules are execution-ready, enabling accurate build, price, configure, and fulfill.Drive a single, scalable model for item and product master data, configuration logic, and impact analysis across engineering and GTM systems.

Other

Product Management, Technology & Organizational Excellence

Build and lead a high-impact product & platform organization spanning product management, platform engineering, architecture, and delivery leadership.Set standards for product discovery, problem framing, roadmap hygiene, prioritization, and outcome measurement—paired with strong engineering practices for execution.Own the engineering effectiveness agenda: developer experience, CI/CD maturity, test automation, reliability/observability, and reduction of technical debt.Partner with Engineering and Enterprise Architecture to manage modernization, scalability, security, resiliency, and platform governance.Champion AI adoption across the product engineering value stream (SDLC and business workflows), including enablement, change management, responsible AI controls, and measurable business outcomes.

Executive Leadership & Influence

Own portfolio-level investment strategy and prioritization, including multi-year funding models, business cases, and ROI tracking.Operate as a trusted peer to senior Engineering, Manufacturing, Sourcing, Product, and Technology leaders, co-owning outcomes rather than serving as a delivery function.Communicate clearly and credibly at the executive level on strategy, roadmap progress, risks, dependencies, and realized enterprise value.Represent the Product Engineering platform strategy in enterprise governance, planning, and transformation forums.

Skills And Minimum Experience Required

15+ years of progressive leadership across product management, engineering/platform leadership, and enterprise technology.Proven ability to define product vision and translate it into roadmaps, operating models, and measurable outcomes across cross-functional stakeholders.Demonstrated experience leading engineering organizations through modern SDLC transformation (CI/CD, automated testing, DevSecOps, reliability/observability) and improving delivery performance.Strong fluency in platform, data, and integration architectures, with the ability to balance speed, quality, risk, and scalability.Executive presence with the ability to influence across business and technology domains and drive adoption at scale.

Preferred Skills And Experience

Experience building and scaling platform product teams (product + engineering) and driving durable adoption across diverse user communities.Hands-on experience applying AI/ML and automation to the SDLC and enterprise workflows (e.g., copilots, test generation, intelligent triage, knowledge management) with responsible AI practices.Familiarity with product lifecycle/product data domains (e.g., PLM/PDM, BOM/change workflows, CAD integrations, PIM/MDM) and the broader engineered-product value stream.Experience integrating product and engineering platforms with ERP, CPQ, Digital Commerce, and MDM ecosystems.MBA or equivalent business leadership experience.Systems thinker who balances long-term platform strategy with engineering and operational realities; comfortable operating in ambiguity and simplifying complex domains.

Travel Requirements

21-40%

The range for this position is $201,000 to $402,000. Placement within this range may vary, depending on the applicant’s experience and geographic location. Acuity offers generous benefits including health care, dental coverage, vision plans, 401K benefits, and commissions/incentive compensation depending on the role. For a list of our benefits, click here.

We value diversity and are an equal opportunity employer.  All qualified applicants will be considered for employment without regards to race, color, age, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity and expression, ethnicity or national origin, disability, pregnancy, religion, covered veteran status, protected genetic information, or any other characteristic protected by law. 

Please click here and here for more information.

companyDescription: WhiteOptics™ develops and manufactures the patent-pending WhiteOptics™ Reflector based on a highly reflective plastic substrate. The reflector is unique in that it has extremely high diffuse reflectance and has greater than 97% reflectance across the visible spectrum, allowing light fixtures to be designed with more efficient light output while the highly diffuse nature of reflection allows the light to be distributed evenly with a low-glare aesthetic.

title: Senior Strategy & Planning Associate
descriptionHtml:
About The Role

Join a high-impact global team at the center of one of Uber's most dynamic and strategic businesses. Uber Delivery is reshaping how people eat, shop, and get what they need, fast. The Global Marketplace & Courier Strategy team drives the reliability, quality, and efficiency behind every order worldwide. We support the Delivery Leadership Team by challenging and changing the trajectory of our marketplace operating point.

We're looking for a senior operator to help identify and execute data-driven strategies to strengthen the Global Delivery marketplace at scale. You'll partner with Regional Operations, Product, Engineering, and Data Science to generate insights, develop frameworks, standardize best practices, and drive systemic improvements across the marketplace experience, including merchant and courier operations, user experience, and defect prevention.

This is a highly cross-functional and analytical role - ideal for someone who combines strong strategic thinking, operational depth, and the ability to influence across global teams to deliver measurable impact for millions of users worldwide.

What You'll Do

  • Lead Global Priorities: Lead special projects to solve emerging global challenges and opportunities, owning everything from problem structuring and data analysis to strategy development, stakeholder alignment, and cross-functional implementation
  • Analytical Decision Making: Lead and perform in-depth analysis to identify root causes of key marketplace challenges (e.g., merchant-driven defects, courier engagement issues, or user experience friction)
  • Global Operations Champion: Partner with Regional Operations leaders to understand local nuances and scale globally-consistent best practices
  • Product and Operations Strategy: Collaborate with Product, Engineering, and CommOps to design and implement systematic solutions, new features, and process improvements to drive a more reliable end-to-end experience
  • Author the Global Playbook: Build frameworks, models, and dashboards to monitor pillar-specific performance, ensuring transparency and accountability across global and regional teams
  • Global Project Management: Own cross-functional governance for your domain, ensuring priorities are resourced, tracked, and embedded into Uber Delivery's long-term product and marketplace roadmap

Basic Qualifications

  • 4+ years of experience in strategy, operations, consulting, analytics, or a related field with a focus on Marketplace businesses
  • Proficiency in SQL and experience using data to identify opportunities, quantify impact, and guide decision-making
  • Bachelor's Degree

Preferred Qualifications

  • Ability to build strong relationships across diverse teams and influence without authority in a highly cross-functional environment
  • Strong communication skills with the ability to simplify complex ideas and align senior partners around a shared strategy
  • High standards and a bias toward action, comfortable working through ambiguity, driving alignment, and holding a high bar for quality and delivery
  • Demonstrated ability to set strategic vision and translate insights into clear executional plans
  • Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with the ability to interpret data, identify root causes, and develop actionable solutions
  • Proven track record of leading complex, cross-functional initiatives with measurable business impact in a global technology company
  • Ability to leverage AI to do more with less (e.g. Cursor, Claude Cowork)
  • Experience with Python is a big plus
  • Passion for improving reliability, efficiency, and user experience through scalable, global solutions
  • Proven regional acumen, experience in working across different countries and cultures

For New York, NY-based roles: The base salary range for this role is USD$131,000 per year - USD$145,500 per year. For San Francisco, CA-based roles: The base salary range for this role is USD$131,000 per year - USD$145,500 per year. For all US locations, you will be eligible to participate in Uber's bonus program, and may be offered an equity award & other types of comp. All full-time employees are eligible to participate in a 401(k) plan. You will also be eligible for various benefits. More details can be found at the following link https://jobs.uber.com/en/benefits., For New York, NY-based roles: The base salary range for this role is USD$131,000 per year - USD$145,500 per year. For San Francisco, CA-based roles: The base salary range for this role is USD$131,000 per year - USD$145,500 per year. For all US locations, you will be eligible to participate in Uber's bonus program, and may be offered an equity award & other types of comp. All full-time employees are eligible to participate in a 401(k) plan. You will also be eligible for various benefits. More details can be found at the following link https://jobs.uber.com/en/benefits.

companyDescription:
We are Uber. The go-getters. The kind of people who are relentless about our mission to help people go anywhere and get anything and earn their way. Movement is what we power. It’s our lifeblood. It runs through our veins. It’s what gets us out of bed each morning. It pushes us to constantly reimagine how we can move better. For you. For all the places you want to go. For all the things you want to get. For all the ways you want to earn. Across the entire world. In real time. At the incredible speed of now.

The idea for Uber was born on a snowy night in Paris in 2008, and ever since then our DNA of reimagination and reinvention carries on. We’ve grown into a global platform powering flexible earnings and the movement of people and things in ever expanding ways. We’ve gone from connecting rides on 4 wheels to 2 wheels to 18-wheel freight deliveries. From takeout meals to daily essentials to prescription drugs to just about anything you need at any time and earning your way. From drivers with background checks to real-time verification, safety is a top priority every single day. At Uber, the pursuit of reimagination is never finished, never stops, and is always just beginning.

title: Freelance Junior Journalist - AI Trainer
descriptionHtml:
Please submit your resume in English and indicate your level of English proficiency.

Mindrift connects specialists with project-based AI opportunities for leading tech companies, focused on testing, evaluating, and improving AI systems. Participation isproject-based, not permanent employment.

What This Opportunity Involves

As an AI Trainer - Junior Journalist, your work will help train AI models, shaping how they understand and generate human-like text. This isn't just traditional writing—you'll be crafting text that teaches AI reasoning, logic accuracy, nuance, and clarity.

While each project involves unique tasks, contributors may:

  • Craft original, clear, and fact-checked responses based on project guidelines.
  • Generate prompts that challenge AI.
  • Define comprehensive scoring criteria to evaluate the accuracy of the AI's answers.
  • Follow style and quality standards to ensure consistency

What We Look For

This opportunity is a good fit if you are seeking for open to part-time, non-permanent projects. Ideally, contributors will have:

  • Bachelor's degree in Journalism, Communications, Linguistics, Literature, or Education to ensure a strong understanding of grammar and stylistic features;
  • At least 1 year of professional experience in Journalism, Communications, PR, etc., with strong skills in critical thinking and working with text in English language;
  • Strong written English (C1/C2);
  • Stable internet connection.

How It Works

Apply → Pass qualification(s) → Join a project → Complete tasks → Get paid

Project time expectations

For this project, tasks are estimated to require around 10-20 hours per week during active phases, based on project requirements. This is an estimate, not a guaranteed workload, and applies only while the project is active.

Compensation

On this project, contributors can earn up to $30 per hour equivalent, depending on their level and pace of contribution.

Compensation varies across projects depending on scope, complexity, and required expertise. Please note that other projects on the platform may offer different earning levels based on their requirements.

companySlogan: Be the “I” in AI with Mindrift. Work from anywhere on groundbreaking AI projects
companyDescription:
Mindrift (mindrift.ai) is the expert-sourcing platform built by Toloka, a Nebius Group (Nasdaq: NBIS) company and a global leader in data services for AI since 2014. Mindrift’s CEO and co-founder, Olga Megorskaya, launched the platform to connect domain experts — from software engineers and physicians to linguists and lawyers — with AI training projects for the world's leading technology companies.

Through Mindrift, over 10,000 professionals across 50+ domains and 15+ languages contribute to supervised fine-tuning, reinforcement learning from human feedback (RLHF), model evaluation, and red-teaming for frontier AI models developed by leading global technology companies.

Operated by Toloka from Amsterdam, Netherlands, Mindrift offers flexible, fully remote, project-based opportunities — empowering experts to shape the future of generative AI, one task at a time.

title: Project Coordinator
descriptionHtml:
Description

WHO WE ARE

Through our offering of furniture, walls, and audio-visual solutions, Continua Interiors is on a mission to transform the way people work, learn and heal across the Midwest. Continua places great importance on fostering a supportive work environment that values culture, community, and connection with both team members and clients. We are not just a company; we are a movement, reshaping the dealer landscape across the Midwest.

OUR CORE VALUES

Strive To Be Great | Do Hard Things | Stronger Together | Own It

Accountabilities

  • Support Project Management
  • Drop Shipments
  • Punch List Resolution
  • Service and Warranty

What You'll Be Doing

Support Project Management

  • Assist project managers with administrative tasks such as preparing project status reports, tracking product shipments, preparing work orders for scheduling, logging change orders and punch list documentation
  • Organize and maintain project files and records
  • Assist in developing project schedules and timelines
  • Communicate proactively with project managers regarding project status, changes, and project risks
  • Address and resolve project-related issues promptly and professionally, as directed by the project manager
  • Assist in resource allocation and scheduling based on project needs

Drop Shipments

  • Distribute shipment notifications to stakeholders for product delivering direct from vendor partners to client locations

Punch List Resolution

  • Support quality assurance efforts by ensuring project deliverables meet quality standards
  • Document outstanding issues, submit claims to vendor partners and direct ordering for replacement products
  • Implement best practices and lessons learned from previous projects
  • Setup work orders for return trips to rectify punch list
  • Prepare final punch list documentation verifying resolution of all outstanding items
  • Drive timely invoicing to meet forecast projections

Service and Warranty

  • Communicate with customers and manufacturers to find repair solutions
  • Collaborate with Service Technicians to diagnose issues and identify needed parts
  • Order parts and labor from vendors for warranty work
  • Keep customers informed about repair status and timelines
  • Schedule repair appointments once parts are confirmed
  • Send reminder communication to customers one day before scheduled service
  • Review and close out completed work orders, ensuring all details are accurate and all repair work is complete
  • Generate and submit daily invoices for vendor reimbursements

What You Should Have

  • Prior experience in project coordination or a related role is beneficial
  • Bachelor’s degree preferred in business, communications, or an industry related field
  • Knowledge of commercial furniture solutions would be an asset
  • Experience with Microsoft Office, Monday.com, Enterprise resource planning (ERP), and an enthusiasm for learning new software programs
  • Ability to manage multiple tasks and projects simultaneously without compromising quality or deadlines
  • Ability to pay close attention to details and ensure accuracy in project documentation and reporting
  • Taking initiative to anticipate project needs and address issues before they become critical
  • Critical thinking and problem solving
  • Excellent communication skills: Listens and gets clarification, responds well to questions, writes clearly and informatively

What We Bring To The Table

  • A State-of-the-Art Facility with incredible technology throughout and a multitude of workspaces at your disposal
  • Use the EOS (Entrepreneurial Operating System) methodology to provide tools to help individuals and teams achieve goals, growth, and improvement.
  • Quarterly All Company Meetings
  • Community Service opportunities
  • Company social hours
  • Ability to use your talents and strengths to make a positive impact

Compensation And Benefits

The hiring compensation range for this position is an hourly rate of $24.04-$27.65/hour. Actual compensation will be determined based on a variety of factors, including but not limited to the candidate's experience, skills, qualifications, and location, as well as internal equity and market considerations.

Benefits Available In This Position Include

  • Medical, Dental, and Vision insurance
  • Company paid Short Term and Long Term Disability insurance
  • Critical Illness and Accident Insurance
  • HSA, DCA and FSA options available
  • Paid time off and holidays
  • Paid Parental Leave
  • 401k Match (50% of every 1% up to 4%)

Disclosures

Notice: The physical demands described above are representative of those that must be met by an employee to successfully perform the essential functions of this job. Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions. The above job description is not intended to be an all-inclusive list of duties and standards of the position. Incumbents will follow any other instructions, and perform any other related duties, as assigned by their manager.

Notice: This job description is not an employment agreement or contract. Management has the exclusive right to alter this job description at any time without notice.

Continua is a drug-free workplace.

Continua is an Equal Employment Opportunity Employer. All qualified candidates will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, national origin, gender, age, religion, sexual orientation, veteran status, or marital status.

companyDescription:
Bringing people together through integrated workplace strategy & hospitality-driven experiences since 1980.

Intereum is proud to help create and inspire spaces to work, learn, heal, and live. Serving proudly as Minnesota's Certified MillerKnoll dealer, we're a full-service resource that provides a comprehensive offering of workplace solutions from commercial furnishings, audio visual technology, and modular walls. 

Following the Entrepreneurial Operating System (EOS) as our business model, we utilize this model to align our vision, core values, and fuel our planned growth. Our core values are the building blocks for our purpose, and we see them at work every day supporting our team members and clients.

As visionaries, Intereum is forging the way in unprecedented healthcare, education, audio visual, and office solutions. Transforming spaces from ordinary to extraordinary, Intereum sees things others can’t see and utilizes tools others don’t have. With business acumen, a rich culture, and an integrated process, Intereum has grown to become a top regional choice for office renovation, reconfiguration, and completely new office environments. 

With a devotion to excellence and creating fully integrated audio visual environments, the Intereum Audio Visual team is committed to taking your space from ordinary to extraordinary in the simplest way possible. Industry expertise coupled with technical knowledge allows the Audio Visual team to provide better designs and engineering that thrive in integrated environments. With intuitive, reliable, and dependable solutions, our team ensures all-inclusive service and support to create work landscapes that can be counted on to perform. Please visit us at www.intereum.com.

title: Freelance Junior Journalist - AI Trainer
descriptionHtml:
Please submit your resume in English and indicate your level of English proficiency.

Mindrift connects specialists with project-based AI opportunities for leading tech companies, focused on testing, evaluating, and improving AI systems. Participation isproject-based, not permanent employment.

What This Opportunity Involves

As an AI Trainer - Junior Journalist, your work will help train AI models, shaping how they understand and generate human-like text. This isn't just traditional writing—you'll be crafting text that teaches AI reasoning, logic accuracy, nuance, and clarity.

While each project involves unique tasks, contributors may:

  • Craft original, clear, and fact-checked responses based on project guidelines.
  • Generate prompts that challenge AI.
  • Define comprehensive scoring criteria to evaluate the accuracy of the AI's answers.
  • Follow style and quality standards to ensure consistency

What We Look For

This opportunity is a good fit if you are seeking for open to part-time, non-permanent projects. Ideally, contributors will have:

  • Bachelor's degree in Journalism, Communications, Linguistics, Literature, or Education to ensure a strong understanding of grammar and stylistic features;
  • At least 1 year of professional experience in Journalism, Communications, PR, etc., with strong skills in critical thinking and working with text in English language;
  • Strong written English (C1/C2);
  • Stable internet connection.

How It Works

Apply → Pass qualification(s) → Join a project → Complete tasks → Get paid

Project time expectations

For this project, tasks are estimated to require around 10-20 hours per week during active phases, based on project requirements. This is an estimate, not a guaranteed workload, and applies only while the project is active.

Compensation

On this project, contributors can earn up to $30 per hour equivalent, depending on their level and pace of contribution.

Compensation varies across projects depending on scope, complexity, and required expertise. Please note that other projects on the platform may offer different earning levels based on their requirements.

companySlogan: Be the “I” in AI with Mindrift. Work from anywhere on groundbreaking AI projects
companyDescription:
Mindrift (mindrift.ai) is the expert-sourcing platform built by Toloka, a Nebius Group (Nasdaq: NBIS) company and a global leader in data services for AI since 2014. Mindrift’s CEO and co-founder, Olga Megorskaya, launched the platform to connect domain experts — from software engineers and physicians to linguists and lawyers — with AI training projects for the world's leading technology companies.

Through Mindrift, over 10,000 professionals across 50+ domains and 15+ languages contribute to supervised fine-tuning, reinforcement learning from human feedback (RLHF), model evaluation, and red-teaming for frontier AI models developed by leading global technology companies.

Operated by Toloka from Amsterdam, Netherlands, Mindrift offers flexible, fully remote, project-based opportunities — empowering experts to shape the future of generative AI, one task at a time.

title: Specialist, Content Developer (English Language Arts)
descriptionHtml:
1. About Our Client:

This organization operates in the educational publishing and curriculum development industry, focusing on creating and maintaining English Language Arts curriculum for grades K–12. It addresses the challenge of providing accurate, aligned, and research-based literacy instruction materials that meet state and national standards, supporting educators and students in achieving effective learning outcomes.


2. About the Opportunity:

The Specialist, Content Developer (English Language Arts) is responsible for maintaining and developing curriculum and assessments for K–12 English Language Arts. This role contributes to the design and refinement of educational content that aligns with evidence-based literacy practices and state standards, directly impacting the quality and effectiveness of instructional materials offered.


3. Responsibilities:

• Communicate with content development, product development agile teams, and product management

• Contribute to the creation and refinement of curriculum development user stories

• Consult with and support teachers in curriculum creation and implementation

• Develop ELA K-12 course components including maps, objectives, learning objects, and activities

• Develop and revise K-5 ELA curriculum and assessments aligned to research-based literacy instruction

• Apply knowledge of the Science of Reading to evaluate instructional materials

• Collaborate with cross-functional Agile teams to complete projects and features

• Work with product development managers to ensure high-quality curricula and resources

• Propose and implement new procedures within own area

• Negotiate resources within and across teams to achieve objectives

• Maintain effective relationships within related areas


4. Requirements:

• Bachelor’s degree required; education, curriculum, or related field preferred

• Minimum 2 years teaching experience; virtual teaching preferred

• Minimum 3 years curriculum development experience; asynchronous virtual learning preferred

• Experience developing or teaching K-5 ELA curricula aligned to the Science of Reading

• Deep understanding of evidence-based literacy instruction and structured literacy approaches

• Strong knowledge of ELA instruction areas including reading, writing, language, and speaking/listening

• Experience creating curriculum integrating literacy skills with authentic texts aligned to standards

• Proven ability to meet deadlines and produce detailed, accurate work

• Proficiency with Microsoft Office, HTML, and capacity to learn new software quickly

• Ability to work independently and collaboratively

• Excellent verbal and written communication skills

• Knowledge of accessibility standards (WCAG 2.2 AA) is a plus


5. Pay Range and Compensation Package:

• The minimum full-time salary range is between $60,000 - $68,000

• Eligible to participate in an annual incentive program


Equal Opportunity Statement: Our client is an equal opportunity employer. They celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, or national origin.


Note:

RemoteHunter is not the Employer of Record (EOR) for this role. Our purpose in this opportunity is to connect exceptional candidates with leading employers. We help job seekers worldwide discover roles that match their goals and guide them to complete their full application directly through the hiring company’s career page or ATS.

title: Claims Assistant
descriptionHtml:
1. About Our Client:


The organization operates nationally within the risk management sector, specializing in solutions for workers’ compensation, auto, health, and disability management. Established in 1987 and publicly traded since 1991, this company addresses challenges in managing claims and risk by investing in technology and human capital to provide integrated services. With a workforce of over 4,000 employees across the United States, it maintains a culture grounded in values such as accountability, commitment, and integrity.


2. About the Opportunity:


The Claims Assistant role supports the claims team by managing the initial setup and ongoing administration of workers’ compensation claims. This position is crucial in maintaining accurate claim records, facilitating communication, and ensuring smooth case management processes, thereby contributing directly to the organization’s ability to serve its clients effectively. The role is fully remote.


3. Responsibilities:


• Set up new claims


• Process mail and handle files prior to paperless transition


• Enter notes and diary entries in the claims system


• Process payments as needed


• Manage form letters, state forms, and reports


• Assist claims examiners with calls involving providers, claimants, and customers


• Perform additional duties as assigned


4. Requirements:


• Excellent written and verbal communication skills


• Proficiency with Microsoft Office (Word, Excel) and general PC literacy


• Ability to work independently and collaboratively


• Strong organizational skills


• High school diploma required; college degree preferred


• Six months of service-oriented office experience preferred


5. Pay Range and Compensation Package:


• Pay range: $16.90 to $23.42 per hour, determined by factors including location, experience, and qualifications


• Salary levels may be adjusted based on assessment during the interview process


6. Benefits & Perks:


• Medical insurance (HDHP) with pharmacy


• Dental insurance


• Vision insurance


• Long Term Disability


• Health Savings Account and Flexible Spending Accounts


• Life Insurance


• Accident and Critical Illness Insurance


• Pre-paid Legal Insurance


• Parking and Transit FSA accounts


• 401(k) and ROTH 401(k)


• Paid time off


Equal Opportunity Statement: Our client is an equal opportunity employer. They celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, or national origin.


Note:

RemoteHunter is not the Employer of Record (EOR) for this role. Our purpose in this opportunity is to connect exceptional candidates with leading employers. We help job seekers worldwide discover roles that match their goals and guide them to complete their full application directly through the hiring company’s career page or ATS.

title: Senior/Principal Product Manager
descriptionHtml:
At Bellota Labs, we are a fast-paced, hypergrowth startup revolutionizing the gaming industry with ClubWPT Gold—an innovative new product for the World Poker Tour. Our mission is fueled by a passion for innovation, game integrity, and delivering exceptional customer experiences. As we scale, we are seeking a Senior Product Manager to shape the future of our consumer-facing products, ensuring they deliver exceptional user experiences and achieve our ambitious goals.


In this role, you will be instrumental in defining the product vision, driving the roadmap for new features, and collaborating with cross-functional teams to deliver results. We're looking for an AI-native PM — someone who uses modern AI tools to move faster, go deeper, and ship more than a traditional PM possibly could. If you are a hands-on product leader with an obsession for user experience, a passion for gaming or poker, and a habit of reaching for AI tools to accelerate every part of your workflow, we want to hear from you.


Key Responsibilities:

  • Write crisp product specifications, including user stories and acceptance criteria, to guide the development process. Use AI tools (e.g., ChatGPT, Claude) to accelerate spec drafting, edge-case analysis, and documentation — while keeping final judgment, framing, and prioritization firmly in your hands

  • Drive the roadmap for assigned features, ensuring alignment with company objectives and timelines

  • Collect and integrate feedback from subject matter experts, internal stakeholders, and users. Use AI to synthesize user interviews, support tickets, reviews, and survey data at scale, surfacing themes and insights faster than manual review allows

  • Conduct research on industry best practices, competitor approaches, and emerging trends to inform product decisions. Leverage AI-powered research tools (e.g., Perplexity, ChatGPT, Claude) to accelerate competitive teardowns, market scans, and trend analysis

  • Collaborate closely with internal design, engineering, and data teams to deliver seamless user experiences. Use AI-assisted prototyping tools (e.g., v0, Lovable, Figma AI, Cursor, Replit Agent) to bring ideas to life quickly and make cross-functional conversations more concrete

  • Analyze product data and user behavior to identify areas for improvement and report actionable findings. Leverage AI-assisted analytics and SQL-generation tools to get to insight faster, without waiting on a data queue

  • Stay hands-on throughout the product lifecycle, from ideation to execution, ensuring quality and performance. Use AI to build lightweight prototypes, mockups, or internal tools that unblock teams and accelerate decisions

  • Champion AI in the product process, continuously evaluating new tools and techniques that can elevate the team's velocity, insight, and quality — and sharing learnings broadly



What We Are Looking For:

  • Experience playing poker (online, card rooms, or tournaments)

  • 4-15 years of experience as a Product Manager or Product Designer in software development

  • Proven experience designing online B2C products, ideally in gaming, poker, or entertainment

  • A strong eye for UI/visual design, with the ability to collaborate effectively with design teams

  • Basic programming knowledge to communicate effectively with engineering teams

  • Hands-on fluency with AI tools in a professional product workflow — using them for research synthesis, spec writing, data analysis, and prototyping — with clear judgment about where AI accelerates the work and where human judgment must lead

  • Passion for poker or video games—or a desire to learn about the gaming industry

  • Exceptional communication and organizational skills, with a focus on driving results

  • Ability to thrive in a fast-paced startup environment and juggle multiple priorities



Nice to Have:

  • Experience conducting user research or testing (including AI-assisted research synthesis)

  • Knowledge of analytics tools to monitor product performance and user behavior, including AI-assisted analytics copilots

  • Familiarity with Agile or Scrum methodologies




At Bellota Labs, we are defining the future of online gaming with cutting-edge products and a team of passionate professionals. You will have the opportunity to shape the direction of our flagship product, work with a world-class team, and make a lasting impact on the gaming industry.


 


Ready to join us on this exciting journey? Apply now and we can build something extraordinary together.


We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

title: Director of Sales, Private Haulers
descriptionHtml:
About Routeware, Inc.

Routeware is the industry leader in providing software and data analysis that enables public and private enterprises to improve the effectiveness of their waste and recycling programs. Our solutions help organizations save time and money, increase efficiency, improve safety and help make our world a greener place.

Based in Portland, Oregon, the company employs a distributed team across North America and the UK. Our brands, products, and services provide an all-in-one platform to municipal and private waste haulers and other fleet services. As part of an essential and growing industry, we seek solution-oriented team players who want to positively impact the environment. Our work environment is collaborative, dynamic, fast-paced, and fun with a strong appreciation for innovation and initiative.

Our Mission

  • Transform waste collection to drive a better future for generations to come.

Our Values

  • Adaptability - The waste industry and technology are ever-changing, and the Routeware team never stops adapting to be at the forefront of technology innovation, supporting our customers to stay ahead.
  • Mission-driven - At Routeware, it matters to us that our work has a lasting positive impact on our customers' outcomes, our fellow team members' well-being, and the long-term sustainability of our environment.
  • Human-first - While technology drives our products; the real, live, caring people at Routeware are the true drivers of meaningful outcomes for our customers.

Position Overview

We are seeking a results-driven Director of Sales, Private Haulers to lead our efforts in expanding us within the private sector. This role requires a strategic leader with a strong background in sales, Pipeline growth and strategic relationship building. The ideal candidate will drive revenue growth by developing targeted sales strategies, optimizing prospecting efforts, and managing key accounts.

Responsibilities

  • Develop and execute a sales strategy to drive new business and expand relationships.
  • Lead and mentor a team of sales professionals, promoting pipeline growth and deal continuance.
  • Establish and refine sales processes tailored to the Private Hauling sector, focusing on prospecting, lead qualification, and contract negotiations.
  • Drive outbound sales efforts, leveraging phone, email, and in-person meetings to engage decision-makers.
  • Build and maintain strong relationships with key stakeholders.
  • Monitor and analyze sales metrics, optimizing strategies to improve conversion rates and revenue.
  • Stay informed on industry trends, funding sources, and government procurement processes to enhance sales effectiveness.
  • Collaborate with marketing and product teams to ensure alignment on messaging, lead generation, and customer needs.
  • Own revenue targets within the Private Hauling segment, providing strategic guidance to meet and exceed goals.

Qualifications

  • Proven experience in a senior sales role focused on private sector sales.
  • Deep understanding of municipal procurement processes, budgeting cycles, and decision-making structures.
  • Strong leadership skills, with the ability to mentor and grow a sales team.
  • Excellent communication and negotiation skills, with the ability to influence stakeholders.
  • Data-driven mindset, leveraging analytics to optimize sales performance and strategy.

Benefits

  • Comprehensive benefits (medical with HSA option, vision, dental, and life insurance)
  • Paid parental leave
  • Medical and Dependent FSA
  • 401K match
  • Unlimited PTO
  • Ten company holidays
  • 1 Volunteer day
  • Summer Friday's

descriptionText:
About Routeware, Inc.

Routeware is the industry leader in providing software and data analysis that enables public and private enterprises to improve the effectiveness of their waste and recycling programs. Our solutions help organizations save time and money, increase efficiency, improve safety and help make our world a greener place.

Based in Portland, Oregon, the company employs a distributed team across North America and the UK. Our brands, products, and services provide an all-in-one platform to municipal and private waste haulers and other fleet services. As part of an essential and growing industry, we seek solution-oriented team players who want to positively impact the environment. Our work environment is collaborative, dynamic, fast-paced, and fun with a strong appreciation for innovation and initiative.

Our Mission

Transform waste collection to drive a better future for generations to come.

Our Values

Adaptability - The waste industry and technology are ever-changing, and the Routeware team never stops adapting to be at the forefront of technology innovation, supporting our customers to stay ahead.Mission-driven - At Routeware, it matters to us that our work has a lasting positive impact on our customers' outcomes, our fellow team members' well-being, and the long-term sustainability of our environment.Human-first - While technology drives our products; the real, live, caring people at Routeware are the true drivers of meaningful outcomes for our customers.

Position Overview

We are seeking a results-driven Director of Sales, Private Haulers to lead our efforts in expanding us within the private sector. This role requires a strategic leader with a strong background in sales, Pipeline growth and strategic relationship building. The ideal candidate will drive revenue growth by developing targeted sales strategies, optimizing prospecting efforts, and managing key accounts.

Responsibilities

Develop and execute a sales strategy to drive new business and expand relationships. Lead and mentor a team of sales professionals, promoting pipeline growth and deal continuance. Establish and refine sales processes tailored to the Private Hauling sector, focusing on prospecting, lead qualification, and contract negotiations. Drive outbound sales efforts, leveraging phone, email, and in-person meetings to engage decision-makers. Build and maintain strong relationships with key stakeholders. Monitor and analyze sales metrics, optimizing strategies to improve conversion rates and revenue. Stay informed on industry trends, funding sources, and government procurement processes to enhance sales effectiveness. Collaborate with marketing and product teams to ensure alignment on messaging, lead generation, and customer needs. Own revenue targets within the Private Hauling segment, providing strategic guidance to meet and exceed goals. 

Qualifications

Proven experience in a senior sales role focused on private sector sales. Deep understanding of municipal procurement processes, budgeting cycles, and decision-making structures. Strong leadership skills, with the ability to mentor and grow a sales team. Excellent communication and negotiation skills, with the ability to influence stakeholders. Data-driven mindset, leveraging analytics to optimize sales performance and strategy. 

Benefits

Comprehensive benefits (medical with HSA option, vision, dental, and life insurance)Paid parental leaveMedical and Dependent FSA401K matchUnlimited PTOTen company holidays1 Volunteer daySummer Friday's

Routeware is an Equal Opportunity Employer and prohibits all forms of discrimination or harassment. At Routeware, we are committed to the principle of equality, and all employment decisions are based on job requirements, business needs, and individual qualifications, without regard to race, color, religion or belief, national, social or ethnic origin, sex (including pregnancy), age, physical, mental or sensory disability, HIV Status, sexual orientation, gender identity and/or expression, marital, civil union or domestic partnership status, past or present military service, family medical history or genetic information, family or parental status, or any other status protected by the laws or regulations in the locations where we operate.
companySlogan: Integrated waste and recycling technology for private haulers and municipalities.
companyDescription:
Routeware helps mid-large private and municipal haulers and cities relieve operational and cost pressures and overcome environmental challenges. More than 1,000 customers throughout North America and the United Kingdom rely on our technology to make trucks safer, optimize routes, streamline operations, and engage and educate communities.

A team of nearly 250 employees works diligently to create the most efficient and effective technologies across every stage of the waste collection lifecycle.

title: Dean of Secondary School
descriptionHtml:
Position Summary

Are you a visionary educator who thrives at the intersection of student support and instructional excellence? Pilgrim School is seeking a Dean of Secondary School to serve as a dynamic leadership partner in driving our ambitious, future-focused vision for learning. This isn't a traditional enforcement role; it is a high-visibility, relational leadership position designed for someone who believes that a thriving school culture is the foundation for academic innovation. As an on-the-ground leader of our division, you will bridge the gap between big-picture strategy and daily classroom reality, ensuring that every student is seen, supported, and challenged to reach their full potential.

Major Areas of Responsibility

  • Act as a primary point of contact for families regarding student conduct and well-being, building trusting partnerships that align the home and school experience.
  • Provide holistic student support by triangulating academic, behavioral, and social-emotional information to design and monitor eff ective student interventions.
  • Lead the daily implementation of the school’s discipline philosophy, ensuring a safe, respectful, and consistent environment through a high-visibility presence in all communal and transitional spaces.
  • Conduct daily classroom walkthroughs and partner with the Director to provide faculty with real-time feedback, ensuring pedagogical standards and student-centered design are executed with consistency.
  • Direct the daily operations of school life in the Secondary School, including announcements, assemblies, attendance, and front-offi ce logistics to maintain a predictable and rigorous divisional rhythm.
  • Serve as a core member of the Secondary Leadership Team, acting as a proxy for the Director and providing critical updates on instructional trends, faculty needs, and divisional health.
  • Teach one class in the Secondary School.

Preferred Background

  • Experience in school leadership or a dean-level role, with a demonstrated track record of managing student conduct and leading teachers in a Secondary School setting.
  • Deep understanding of the unique developmental needs of middle school and high school students.
  • Excellent pedagogical skills and ability to design engaging, project-based units.
  • Ability to model high-level pedagogy as a classroom teacher and provide formative coaching that helps faculty improve their practice in real-time.
  • Exceptional written and verbal communication skills, specifi cally in navigating complex or sensitive conversations with parents and faculty.
  • Experience working in schools that utilize innovative instructional models, such as project-based learning or design thinking.
  • Ability to manage complex logistics while keeping the "human element" of the school community at the center.
  • A Master’s degree in Education, School Leadership, or a related fi eld.

If you do not meet all qualifications but feel you are a good fi t for this position, we welcome your submission.

Salary Range And Benefits

The salary range for this position is $83,000-$105,000 depending on experience and qualifications. We off er a full and robust range of medical, dental and vision benefits, participation in a 403b retirement plan, which we will match up to 4%, and life insurance. More information about all of our benefits will be provided during the interview process.

Timeline and Start Date

This position will remain open until filled. The start date for this position is August 1, 2026. Ideally, the successful candidate would also be able to participate in our leadership work from June 22-30, but is not a requirement. Please note that employment is subject to Live Scan clearance and reference checks prior to beginning employment.

How To Apply

To be fully considered for the position, please send your resume, cover letter, and references (including a supervisor) to employment@pilgrim-school.org.

Pilgrim School provides equal employment opportunities (EEO) to all employees and applicants for employment without regard to race, color, religion, gender, national origin, age, disability or genetics. In addition to federal law requirements, Pilgrim School complies with applicable state and local laws governing nondiscrimination in employment.

title: Patient Care Advisor - Remote
descriptionHtml:
1. About Our Client:

The organization is a Federally Qualified, Non-Profit Health Center providing comprehensive primary, preventative, and behavioral health services to diverse communities across several counties. It focuses on delivering care to underserved and vulnerable populations regardless of insurance status or ability to pay.


2. About the Opportunity:

The Patient Care Advisor serves as the primary contact for current and potential patients, coordinating appointment scheduling across medical, mental health, and COVID-related services. This role ensures efficient patient access to care through effective communication and management of incoming calls, playing a key part in facilitating patient-centered service delivery.


3. Responsibilities:

• Answer all incoming external and internal calls.

• Schedule appointments for all clinic sites using the electronic health record system.

• Update patient demographic information as needed.

• Conduct outreach calls to patients who missed appointments and other outbound communications.

• Provide clerical support including message taking and word processing.

• Support peer training for new call center staff on appointment scheduling and services.

• Conduct calls following quality and procedural standards; assist in continuous quality improvement activities.

• Respond constructively to feedback and coaching.

• Adhere to standard operating procedures and demonstrate reliability.

• Provide coverage for other departments as directed.

• Perform additional duties as required.


4. Requirements:

• Minimum two years of experience in a service-related role; call center or healthcare customer service experience preferred.

• High school diploma or equivalent required; Associate Degree preferred.

• Strong communication and interpersonal skills, including clarity in phone interactions.

• Proficiency with computer systems such as Microsoft Word, Excel, PowerPoint, and scheduling software; ability to meet keyboarding standards.

• Organizational skills with ability to manage tasks and anticipate team needs based on volume trends.

• Ability to build and maintain positive customer and employee relationships.

• Demonstrated leadership and team motivation skills.

• Bilingual communication skills preferred.


5. Pay Range and Compensation Package:

• Salary range: $20.00 to $23.13 per hour.


Equal Opportunity Statement: Our client is an equal opportunity employer. They celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, or national origin.


Note:

RemoteHunter is not the Employer of Record (EOR) for this role. Our purpose in this opportunity is to connect exceptional candidates with leading employers. We help job seekers worldwide discover roles that match their goals and guide them to complete their full application directly through the hiring company’s career page or ATS.

title: Crew Member
descriptionHtml:
Why Wendy's

WHY WENDY’S?

As a Crew Member at Wendy’s, you’re part of the family from Day 1.

We get you. We got you. Here’s what you can expect as a Crew Member on our team:

  • Perks – FREE MEALS while you’re working, 401(k) and employee assistance program for all employees. Plus PTO and healthcare benefits for qualifying employees
  • Career Growth – Wanna move up? We have opportunities to grow within and beyond our restaurants
  • Flexible schedule – Full-time, or just a few hours a week? We have a schedule to fit your needs
  • Paid Training – First job? Never worked in a restaurant? Our comprehensive training program will make sure you’re ready to deliver great food and great service

Responsibilities

What We Expect From You

What you bring to the table:

  • A positive attitude; you always pitch in and help your fellow crew and customers
  • You want to learn something new and be a part of something good
  • If something doesn't seem right, you make it right

Pay Range: $14.00 - $16.73

What we expect from you

You Must Be Willing And Able To

  • Stand and move for most - if not all - of your shift
  • Use restaurant equipment such as a headset, register or grill
  • Follow brand standards and guidelines to prepare great food, serve our customers and maintain a clean restaurant that you’re proud to work in

For more information on specific equipment or job requirements, see the job description or talk to the hiring manager.

Real food. Real people.

We are a proud equal opportunity employer - all ages, races, religions, nationalities, genders and the LGBTQ+ community are welcome. We provide reasonable accommodations in order to enable people with disabilities to perform the essential functions of their jobs.Real food. Real people.

Wendy’s has an ongoing need for this role

title:
Recruiting Coordinator/OperationsNew
San Francisco
descriptionHtml:
fal is the generative media ecosystem powering the next generation of AI products. We build the infrastructure, tools, and model access that teams need to move from idea to production, and do it at scale without compromise. For developers and enterprises, fal is the foundation that makes generative media not just possible, but practical: a unified platform where high-performance inference, orchestration, and observability come together to unlock new categories of AI-native products.

As generative media reshapes industries across a market projected to grow by hundreds of billions over the next decade, fal is becoming the ecosystem that ambitious teams build on.

About The Role

We're hiring a Recruiting Coordinator/Operations to help build a best-in-class recruiting engine as we scale. This is a coordination-heavy role, but the scope goes beyond scheduling — you'll own the systems and processes that keep the team organized and moving, and you'll be expected to improve them over time.

You'll help build the processes, documentation, and tooling that grow with us, not just execute the ones that already exist.

Recruiting Operations

  • Maintain data integrity across our ATS (Ashby) and recruiting tools, auditing regularly to keep information accurate.
  • Track interview feedback completion and follow up to ensure SLA’s are met.
  • Keep recruiting documentation and Slack channels up to date.
  • Track and analyze key recruiting metrics (time-to-fill, interview-to-offer ratio, source effectiveness) and bring data-driven recommendations to the team.
  • Identify process gaps and propose improvements.
  • Contribute to broader recruiting initiatives: employer branding, onboarding improvements, and etc.

Candidate Scheduling & Experience

  • Own end-to-end interview scheduling across candidates, hiring managers, and interviewers — managing conflicts, logistics, and every touchpoint.
  • Manage high-volume interview coordination across multiple time zones, calendars, and stakeholders while maintaining a world-class candidate experience.
  • Coordinate all interview logistics, from travel and expenses to room setup and technical support.
  • Serve as the primary point of contact for candidates from initial screening through offer, communicating in a warm, professional, and timely manner.
  • Anticipate scheduling bottlenecks: monitor interviewer availability, flag conflicts early, and keep hiring velocity high.
  • Use Ashby's scheduling tools to automate where possible and reduce manual work.

About You

  • Based in SF and available to work onsite 5 days a week.
  • 2+ years of experience as a Recruiting Coordinator or in a similar role, with a track record of working independently.
  • Prior experience in a SaaS or tech startup environment strongly preferred.
  • Comfortable in fast-moving, ambiguous environments where priorities shift.
  • Proficient in Ashby with strong data hygiene habits and comfort pulling pipeline reports.
  • Strong interpersonal skills — you build trust quickly with hiring managers, candidates, and cross-functional partners.
  • Exceptionally organized and detail-oriented, able to juggle multiple open roles without dropping the ball.
  • You have a do-what-it-takes mindset — proactive, detail-oriented, and dependable, with the ability to anticipate needs, take initiative, and ensure nothing falls through the cracks without needing direction.

What We Offer At Fal

  • Interesting and challenging work
  • Competitive salary and equity
  • A lot of learning and growth opportunities
  • We are currently hiring in downtown San Francisco.
  • We offer visa sponsorship and will help you relocate to San Francisco.
  • Health, dental, and vision insurance (US)
  • Regular team events and offsites

Compensation

  • $110,000-130,000 + equity + comprehensive benefits package

title: Business Development Manager
descriptionHtml:
Authsignal is an industry leader specializing in customer identity and authentication. We help enterprise and mid-market businesses rapidly deploy optimized customer flows that enable a flexible, risk-based approach to authentication — including passkeys, passwordless authentication, identity verification, and biometric authentication.


Over the past year, we experienced exceptional growth and success. In 2026, we are expanding our sales team to support Authsignal's continued momentum. This is a ground-floor opportunity to join an exciting, innovative company at an early stage.


The Opportunity


This is a pure outbound new business role — no account management, no inherited pipeline, no safety net.


You'll be selling into organizations that have never heard of Authsignal, educating the market, opening doors, and converting early conviction into long-term customers. In doing so, you'll play a direct role in shaping our North American revenue engine and go-to-market strategy.


The Role

Based in the greater DFW area, this is 90%+ outbound. You'll own the full sales cycle from first conversation to close, with the majority of your pipeline self-generated through cold calling, LinkedIn, email, and whatever else you can leverage. The occasional warm inbound lead will come your way — but your success will be built on your ability to hunt.


You're someone who genuinely enjoys the grind: networking, reaching out, picking up the phone, and turning cold prospects into real opportunities. Every no is a step closer to a yes, and you wouldn't have it any other way.


This role demands a high level of initiative and resilience. You're comfortable with ambiguity, adapt quickly to change, and don't need a playbook to get moving. You'll attend local and national events with a moderate level of travel, and you'll be measured on one thing: growing Authsignal's business in North America.


What You'll Do

  • Play a key role in the sales team and report to the VP of Revenue and Growth.
  • Be a foundation hire for North America, leveraging existing customers and success to increase market visibility across Texas and the broader US.
  • Prospect and engage new leads weekly to build a consistent pipeline through cold calling, LinkedIn, and partner and industry relationships.
  • Manage a high volume of outbound activity while maintaining a strong presence at local meetups, industry events, and with key partners to build brand awareness.
  • Manage a CRM pipeline in HubSpot, track activity, and achieve quarterly quota targets — including closing assigned inbound leads from time to time.
  • Attend local and national trade shows and conferences.
  • Become a subject matter expert and evangelist of the Authsignal platform, developing in-depth knowledge of industry standards, terminology, and use cases.
  • Interface with key decision-makers and senior leaders to uncover pain points and deliver compelling, tailored presentations.
  • Play a key role in shaping our product roadmap by communicating customer requirements to the product and engineering teams.


Who You Are

  • Comfortable having conversations at a senior or C-level.
  • Confident working with mid-market and enterprise opportunities.
  • Existing relationships in the Texas fintech, banking, payments, fraud, or identity ecosystem are a major advantage.
  • Able to travel 15–30% for trade shows, conferences, and client meetings.
  • Experienced managing 3–12 month sales cycles and comfortable closing 5- to 6-figure deals.
  • 2+ years of experience in SaaS sales, business development, and/or strategic partnerships with financial institutions.
  • Background in payments, cybersecurity, customer identity, or authentication is highly valuable.
  • Strong understanding of modern lead generation concepts and methods.
  • Bachelor's degree in business or a related field.
  • Highly motivated, able to work autonomously in a fast-paced environment, and take full ownership of the role.
  • Strong verbal, written, and presentation skills.


Benefits

  • Base salary + uncapped commission
  • Full benefits package (health, dental, vision, 401k)
  • Based in Dallas-Fort Worth (hybrid, you must live in or relocate to the DFW area)
  • A ground-floor opportunity to join a fast-growing, innovative technology company selling into global markets

This is a pure outbound new business role — no account management, no inherited pipeline, no safety net.
You'll be selling into organizations that have never heard of Authsignal, educating the market, opening doors, and converting early conviction into long-term customers. In doing so, you'll play a direct role in shaping our North American revenue engine and go-to-market strategy.
The RoleBased in the greater DFW area, this is 90%+ outbound. You'll own the full sales cycle from first conversation to close, with the majority of your pipeline self-generated through cold calling, LinkedIn, email, and whatever else you can leverage. The occasional warm inbound lead will come your way — but your success will be built on your ability to hunt.
You're someone who genuinely enjoys the grind: networking, reaching out, picking up the phone, and turning cold prospects into real opportunities. Every no is a step closer to a yes, and you wouldn't have it any other way.
This role demands a high level of initiative and resilience. You're comfortable with ambiguity, adapt quickly to change, and don't need a playbook to get moving. You'll attend local and national events with a moderate level of travel, and you'll be measured on one thing: growing Authsignal's business in North America.
What You'll DoPlay a key role in the sales team and report to the VP of Revenue and Growth.Be a foundation hire for North America, leveraging existing customers and success to increase market visibility across Texas and the broader US.Prospect and engage new leads weekly to build a consistent pipeline through cold calling, LinkedIn, and partner and industry relationships.Manage a high volume of outbound activity while maintaining a strong presence at local meetups, industry events, and with key partners to build brand awareness.Manage a CRM pipeline in HubSpot, track activity, and achieve quarterly quota targets — including closing assigned inbound leads from time to time.Attend local and national trade shows and conferences.Become a subject matter expert and evangelist of the Authsignal platform, developing in-depth knowledge of industry standards, terminology, and use cases.Interface with key decision-makers and senior leaders to uncover pain points and deliver compelling, tailored presentations.Play a key role in shaping our product roadmap by communicating customer requirements to the product and engineering teams.
Who You AreComfortable having conversations at a senior or C-level.Confident working with mid-market and enterprise opportunities.Existing relationships in the Texas fintech, banking, payments, fraud, or identity ecosystem are a major advantage.Able to travel 15–30% for trade shows, conferences, and client meetings.Experienced managing 3–12 month sales cycles and comfortable closing 5- to 6-figure deals.2+ years of experience in SaaS sales, business development, and/or strategic partnerships with financial institutions.Background in payments, cybersecurity, customer identity, or authentication is highly valuable.Strong understanding of modern lead generation concepts and methods.Bachelor's degree in business or a related field.Highly motivated, able to work autonomously in a fast-paced environment, and take full ownership of the role.Strong verbal, written, and presentation skills.
BenefitsBase salary + uncapped commissionFull benefits package (health, dental, vision, 401k)Based in Dallas-Fort Worth (hybrid, you must live in or relocate to the DFW area)A ground-floor opportunity to join a fast-growing, innovative technology company selling into global markets
jobPosterTitle:
Authsignal - Consumer Identity and Authentication. 
Building trust for the world's leading consumer businesses.
companySlogan: Secure customer journeys with Authsignal. Drop flexible authentication flows into user journeys, and stop fraud today.
companyDescription:
Secure your customer journeys with Authsignal. Drop flexible authentication flows into your customer journeys today. Deploy passkeys and out-of-the-box passwordless (MFA) authentication in hours.  

Authsignal is a drop-in authentication platform. Focused on enabling enterprise and mid-market businesses to seamlessly authenticate customer identity, mitigate fraud, and secure customer data.

Partnering with modern consumer-focused enterprises, Authsignal enables a flexible and contextual approach to authentication. Underpinned by a powerful no-code-rules engine

Product and Fraud teams leverage Authsignal to secure customer flows, reduce customer friction, and mitigate risk and fraud. 

Built to drop into any identity stack, out-of-the-box integrations via OpenID Connect (OIDC), Microsoft Azure b2c, ForgeRock, and Ping Identity deliver rapid deployment times and significant cost savings.


Customer service and fraud teams gain complete observability of their customer journeys with a single view of customer actions and audit trail reporting dashboards. Authsignal’s identity stream delivers a real-time view of actions and behavior.

Authsignal enables businesses to easily deploy passwordless authentication, eKYC, biometric authentication, customer journey orchestration, fraud detection, and mitigation powered by a no-code fraud rules engine for rapid policy deployment.





Mitigate risk and fraud with a no-code-rules engine and deliver seamless customer experiences with a hosted and fully customizable UI. 
www.authsignal.com



Authsignal makes it easy to protect customer data, deliver seamless customer authentication experiences

title: IT Operations Intern - NEO
descriptionHtml:
About The Job

Louis Vuitton is seeking an IT Operations Intern to join our NEO (IT) team in New York City. As an IT Operations Intern, you will have the opportunity to learn and contribute to the administration, maintenance, and continuous improvement of the configuration management database (CMDB) within the IT Operations function. This role is crucial for ensuring the integrity, accuracy, and completeness of configuration item (CI) data and CI relationships across infrastructure, applications, network, cloud, and end-user environments.

The IT Operations Intern will support operational effectiveness by providing accurate configuration data for incident management, problem management, change management, asset visibility, service mapping, and audit readiness. This position involves close collaboration with IT Operations, Infrastructure, Service Desk, Application Support, Security, and Asset Management teams to maintain a reliable system of record for technology services and their dependencies.

Job responsibilities

Responsibilities include but are not limited to:

  • Configuration Item Management: Assist with creating, updating, and retiring configuration item records in accordance with established procedures, ensuring data is complete, accurate, and current. Perform regular data quality reviews to identify and resolve inconsistencies and maintain a reliable CMDB.
  • Configuration Management Support: Support the execution and continuous improvement of configuration management policies, standards, and procedures. Assist in maintaining documentation for CMDB processes and help onboard new services and infrastructure components into the CMDB.
  • IT Operations Assistance: Provide CMDB support for IT Operations during incidents, problems, and change management activities. Assist in identifying service dependencies for root cause analysis and enable change impact assessments by ensuring documented relationships between systems are current.
  • Data Reconciliation and Integration: Review automated discovery data and assist with the reconciliation of information from various source systems to validate alignment with CMDB records. Work with technical teams to improve integration between the CMDB and related operational tools.
  • Reporting, Governance, and Compliance: Produce regular reports and dashboards on CMDB health, completeness, and data quality trends. Support audit, risk, and compliance activities by ensuring accurate configuration records and participate in governance reviews.
  • Specific Functions Required Within the IT Operations Team: Supports with maintaining the CMDB as a trusted operational source, ensuring all IT assets are accurately recorded and supporting critical IT processes such as incident, problem, and change management through reliable data. They validate discovery outputs, coordinate data correction, and drive remediation of data quality issues to standardize CI information and support operational readiness.

Profile

We are excited to speak to candidates who possess strong analytical and problem-solving skills, coupled with a solid understanding of Microsoft Office Suite. Proficiency in Microsoft Power Automate, Power BI, and ServiceNow is highly preferred. The ability to collaborate effectively and communicate clearly is essential.

Compensation

  • Undergraduate Interns will be compensated at: $20/hour
  • Graduate Interns will be compensated at $25/hour

Criteria

  • Currently enrolled undergraduate/graduate student pursuing a degree in related field of study
  • Able and willing to work at assigned location or able and willing to relocate to NYC
  • We welcome candidates that can commit to 6 months on a full-time basis for up to 37.5 hours per week starting in June, 2026.
  • Legally authorized to work in the US

The position requires 4-5 days per week on site in the Louis Vuitton Corporate Office, located in the heart of Midtown, Manhattan.

Additional information

Louis Vuitton is a company that respects the uniqueness of each employee and offers everyone the means to find their place and prosper. We promote initiatives aimed at supporting professional equality for everyone. We strive to go above and beyond purely symbolic measures by building a culture passionate about meaningful strategies aimed at crafting an inclusive workforce.

In addition to a generous benefits package, unparalleled career development opportunities, both locally & globally, as an employee at Louis Vuitton, you can expect to be provided with industry leading training which will offer you an in-depth insight into the luxury and retail environment.

Reference LVM32654

title: Entry Level Project Coordinator
descriptionHtml:
Job Title: Entry Level Project Coordinator

Location: Atlanta, GA

Job Type: Full-Time

Note On-campus work in Atlanta, GA

Job Summary

We are seeking a highly organized and motivated Entry Level Project Coordinator to join our dynamic team. This position is ideal for recent graduates or professionals looking to start their career in project management and coordination. The Entry Level Project Coordinator will play a crucial role in supporting project managers to ensure projects are delivered on time, within scope, and within budget.

Key Responsibilities

  • Assist project managers in planning and executing projects from initiation to completion.
  • Coordinate and schedule project meetings, including preparing agendas and taking meeting minutes.
  • Track project progress and update project schedules using project management tools.
  • Communicate with team members and stakeholders to ensure alignment and resolve issues.
  • Prepare and maintain project documentation, reports, and presentations.

Qualifications

  • Bachelor's degree in Business Administration, Management, or a related field.
  • Strong organizational and time management skills with attention to detail.
  • Excellent written and verbal communication skills.
  • Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite (Word, Excel, PowerPoint) and familiarity with project management software.
  • Ability to multitask and prioritize tasks effectively in a fast-paced environment.
